		<article class="npc-card" data-zone="cultist-island" data-search="cult archers ranged combat caligo mutation">
		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">
			<img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/elucidate_idle_cultist_archer_npc_down.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Cult Archers">
			<div class="npc-zone-tag">Cultist Territory</div>
		  </div>
		  <div class="npc-body">
			<div class="npc-role">Ranged Combatants · Precision Units</div>
			<div class="npc-name">Cult Archers</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-short">"Distance does not protect you."</div>
			<div class="npc-faction-row">
			  <span class="npc-faction-tag">Cult of Caligo</span>
			</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-full">
			  <p>Adapted for ranged combat with heightened perception.</p>
			  <p><strong>Connection to Caligo:</strong> Enhanced senses.</p>
			  <p><strong>Connection to Lumen:</strong> Rejected subjects.</p>
			  <p><strong>Behavior:</strong> Aggressive at range.</p>
			  <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Ranged enemies.</p>
			</div>
		  </div>
		</article>
		
		<article class="npc-card" data-zone="cultist-island" data-search="cult leader beholder of fragments of caligo">
		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">
			<img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/elucidate_idle_cult_leader_npc_down.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Cult Leader">
			<div class="npc-zone-tag">Cultist Territory - Fortress</div>
		  </div>
		  <div class="npc-body">
			<div class="npc-role">Ranged Combatants · Precision Units</div>
			<div class="npc-name">Cult Leader</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-short">"You were nine years old when the Church expelled you from the program. You were not told why. ."</div>
			<div class="npc-faction-row">
			  <span class="npc-faction-tag">Cult of Caligo</span>
			</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-full">
			  <p>The Cult Leader stands as the central figure of the Cultist Island, not as a traditional ruler, but as a surviving product of the same system that created every other exile. Unlike the others, however, the Leader retained clarity during the early exposure to experimental procedures tied to Vindication Lumen. While most subjects broke, fragmented, or became unstable, the Cult Leader adapted. This adaptation did not grant power in the conventional sense, but awareness. He became capable of perceiving the structure behind the experiments, the intention of the Church, and the silent presence of Caligo that existed before both.</p>

			  <p>Originally classified as a high-potential subject within the Vindication Lumen program, the Cult Leader was subjected to repeated cognitive trials designed to measure resistance against non-linear perception. Records describe him as "non-compliant but stable," a rare combination that made him both valuable and dangerous. When the Church determined that his interpretations deviated from acceptable doctrine, he was marked for disposal. Instead of termination, he was exiled along with other failed subjects. This decision would later become one of the Church’s most critical errors, as it allowed the formation of a unified group of rejected individuals under a single coherent perspective.</p>

			  <p><strong>Connection to Caligo:</strong> The Cult Leader does not worship Caligo in the same way lesser cultists do. He understands Caligo as a constant, not a deity. To him, Caligo is the condition of existence itself, a force that reveals truth by removing illusions rather than granting meaning. His prolonged exposure allowed him to survive without losing identity, but at the cost of perceiving reality in a way that isolates him from normal human understanding. He teaches others not to seek Caligo, but to endure it. This philosophy shapes the Cult into a group focused on survival rather than blind devotion.</p>

			  <p><strong>Connection to Vindication Lumen:</strong> The Cult Leader represents the system’s greatest failure. Vindication Lumen was designed to create controlled enlightenment, individuals who could process higher knowledge without destabilization. Instead, it produced individuals who could not be controlled. The Leader is proof that the system does not produce obedience, only awareness. His existence challenges the Church’s belief that knowledge can be contained, regulated, or purified. Every action he takes is, in effect, a contradiction of the Church’s purpose.</p>

			  <p><strong>Behavior Toward the Player:</strong> The Cult Leader does not immediately engage in hostility. His interaction with the player depends on accumulated choices, especially those affecting Mind State and previous encounters with cult members. If the player approaches as a soldier of the Church, the Leader responds with controlled resistance and strategic combat. If the player shows hesitation, listens to cult narratives, or accumulates higher Mind State, the Leader shifts into dialogue-driven interaction. He evaluates rather than reacts. To him, the player is not an enemy but a variable, a possible continuation of the same system that created him.</p>

			  <p><strong>Gameplay Relevance:</strong> The Cult Leader functions as a major decision point and narrative pivot. He is encountered during late-stage progression within the Cultist path and acts as either a boss encounter or a dialogue-driven convergence point depending on player alignment. His presence directly influences endings tied to Caligo acceptance or rejection. Combat against him involves layered mechanics tied to Mind State, where higher values alter his attack patterns and dialogue interruptions. If engaged peacefully, he unlocks alternative narrative branches and reveals hidden truths about the origin of both the Church and the experiments.</p>

			  <p><strong>Role in World Events:</strong> The Cult Leader is the reason the Cult persists as an organized faction rather than scattered survivors. He transformed isolated rejection into collective identity. His influence extends beyond the island through indirect effects, including increased Church surveillance, expansion of experimental programs, and the emergence of higher-level Caligo manifestations. His continued existence destabilizes the balance between the Church and the unknown, making him one of the most important figures in the entire Elucidate world.</p>
			</div>
		  </div>
		</article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="theocratic-capital" data-search="Envoy Seven Aldric church operative secret mission higher authority envoy emissary">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://png.pngtree.com/png-vector/20251121/ourmid/pngtree-gray-human-silhouette-on-white-background-flat-colors-empty-avatar-icon-png-image_18034377.webp" alt="NPC Portrait — Envoy Seven, Aldric">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Theocratic Capital</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Church Envoy · Classified Operative</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Envoy Seven — Aldric</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"There are seven of us. I am the last one still assigned. You should ask yourself why that is."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Church of Lumen</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Classified Rank</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Envoy Program</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p>The Church's Envoy Program is not documented in any public-facing Church literature. It exists in two internal records: a resource allocation ledger that codes its operatives as maintenance costs, and a mission archive sealed under Father Calum's personal authorization. Aldric holds the designation Envoy Seven — which means six other Envoys were assigned before him, and he is the only one still operational. He does not explain what happened to the others. He does not need to. The designation itself is the explanation.</p>
              <p>Aldric moves through the Theocratic Capital in the role of a senior administrative attaché — a man who attends meetings, carries documents, and is never quite the most important person in any room. This is by design. His actual function is the delivery of Father Calum's off-record instructions to agents and operatives who cannot be seen receiving those instructions through official channels. He is the Church's deniability infrastructure made into a person.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Higher Authority:</strong> Direct and singular — he reports to Father Calum alone, bypassing every other layer of Church hierarchy. Other Church officials are aware of his existence but not his function. The Draft Officer, for instance, has seen Aldric in the corridors of the Theocratic Capital for years and has registered him as "one of the administrative people" without ever learning his role.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to the World:</strong> Aldric's mission, as fragments of dialogue reveal, involves locating and monitoring the three Fragments of Caligo before Father Calum's consolidation plan moves into its final phase. He has been tracking the Fragment currently in the Merchant Guild's possession for eleven months. He is very close to it. So is Konrad. They have not yet recognized each other.</p>
              <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Encountered in the Theocratic Capital as a background NPC with optional triggered dialogue. Full lore only accessible if the player investigates the Cathedral Archive and cross-references the personnel ledger. One of the few NPCs who, upon recognizing the player's investigation depth, will voluntarily provide a warning rather than an obstacle — because his mission and the player's convergence at a certain point becomes, for Aldric, a variable he prefers to manage rather than eliminate.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>

		<article class="npc-card" data-zone="cultist-island" data-search="eldritch manifestations high mind state entities horror">
		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">
			<img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-elucidate_idle_amalgamated_villagers_npc_right.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Eldritch Manifestations">

		<article class="npc-card" data-zone="lab" data-search="medical staff experiments lumen lab subjects control">
		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">
			<img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/elucidate_idle_church_medical_staff_npc_down.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Church Medical Staff">
			<div class="npc-zone-tag">Laboratory</div>
		  </div>
		  <div class="npc-body">
			<div class="npc-role">Researchers · Experiment Overseers</div>
			<div class="npc-name">Church Medical Staff</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-short">"We are fixing what the world broke."</div>
			<div class="npc-faction-row">
			  <span class="npc-faction-tag">Vindication Lumen</span>
			</div>
			<div class="npc-lore-full">
			  <p>Responsible for subject experimentation and Lumen integration.</p>
			  <p><strong>Connection to Lumen:</strong> Direct implementation.</p>
			  <p><strong>Connection to Caligo:</strong> Studied as threat.</p>
			  <p><strong>Behavior:</strong> Detached.</p>
			  <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Lore and lab encounters.</p>
			</div>
		  </div>
		</article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="outskirts" data-search="elves race ancient forest lumen caligo disconnected mysterious silent">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://png.pngtree.com/png-vector/20251121/ourmid/pngtree-gray-human-silhouette-on-white-background-flat-colors-empty-avatar-icon-png-image_18034377.webp" alt="NPC Portrait — Elven Kind">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Outskirts · Forest Borders</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Race · Ancient People</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Elves</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"They were here before the Church named the light. They will be here after it is renamed again."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Independent</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Pre-Lumen</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Forest Borders</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p>The Elves predate the Church's founding by an era that Church historians have quietly ceased to enumerate. They do not worship Lumen. They do not venerate Caligo. They remember both forces from before either had a name, and that memory makes them difficult to convert and impossible to fully control — two qualities the Church finds indistinguishable from hostility. They have been categorized as "non-aligned indigenous peoples" in official Church records, a classification that manages to say very little while implying a great deal.</p>
              <p>Scattered now across forest margins and territorial boundaries the Church does not officially claim, they persist in small communities that have learned to remain precisely invisible. They know what Caligo is. They have their own word for it, older than the Church's word, and they do not use either word in front of strangers. Whether this constitutes wisdom or simply caution depends entirely on who is asking.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Caligo:</strong> Ambiguous by design. Their long presence near Caligo-adjacent spaces has produced neither corruption nor devotion — only a practiced coexistence that both the Church and the Cult interpret as evidence of their respective theories about the dark.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Lumen:</strong> Absent. They were not present at the Vindication Lumen. They did not build their identity around it. The Church views this as a theological deficiency. The Elves view the Church's view as a relatively recent problem.</p>
              <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Peripheral NPC encounters near forest environments. Provide contextual lore about pre-Church history through dialogue fragments. May offer rare trade items to characters who approach without Church insignia visible.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="outskirts" data-search="dwarfs race craft forge underground ancient materials smith guild stone">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://png.pngtree.com/png-vector/20251121/ourmid/pngtree-gray-human-silhouette-on-white-background-flat-colors-empty-avatar-icon-png-image_18034377.webp" alt="NPC Portrait — Dwarven Kind">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Underground · Forge Districts</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Race · Craftspeople</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Dwarfs</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"They built things that lasted. The Church borrowed the structures and called them holy. The Dwarfs called them foundations."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Independent</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Merchant-Adjacent</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Ancient Craft</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p>Dwarf communities occupy the underground systems beneath several major regions — tunnel networks that predate the Church's surface infrastructure and that the Church has never fully mapped, partially because the maps are written in a Dwarf script that resists Church archival methods, and partially because no Church survey team sent into the deeper tunnels has returned with a complete survey. The Church has categorized this as a geological problem. Experienced soldiers categorize it differently.</p>
              <p>Their craft tradition is the most technically advanced in the world's recorded history. Weapons produced in Dwarf forges use materials sourced from geological strata that no surface mine can reach — ores with properties that Church metallurgists have not been able to reproduce. Blacksmith Conrad's most reliable tools were made by a Dwarf supplier three generations back. He has been unable to replace them.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Ancient Materials:</strong> Some Dwarf-forged objects carry faint Caligo-resonant properties — not corrupted, but attuned, the way stone carries the memory of pressure. Church scholars dispute whether this constitutes contamination or simply the consequence of deep-earth proximity. Dwarf craftspeople decline to engage with this theological debate.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Lumen:</strong> Transactional. They trade with Church-affiliated merchants when pricing is favorable and avoid Church-affiliated merchants when it is not. They are the only race the Church has not been able to absorb through either conversion or economic dependency, because they control the only resource channels the Church cannot replicate.</p>
              <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Specialized traders and craftspeople in Walled City's lower market and certain underground segments. Offer unique weapon upgrades and material trades unavailable elsewhere. Will not barter with characters carrying Church insignia at the highest tier.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="port-city" data-search="pirates rocky shore coastal faction sea independent church authority harbor smugglers">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://cdn-icons-png.flaticon.com/512/48/48848.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Pirates of the Rocky Shore">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Rocky Shore · Port Borders</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Coastal Faction · Independent Operators</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Pirates of the Rocky Shore</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"The Church's jurisdiction ends at the waterline. We begin there."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Independent</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Anti-Church</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Rocky Shore</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p>The Pirates of the Rocky Shore are the only coastal faction operating outside both Church contract and Merchant Guild registration. They exist in the legal negative space between Port City's commercial infrastructure and the open sea — a negative space the Church has repeatedly attempted to close and repeatedly failed to close, because eliminating the Pirates would require a naval force the Church does not have and a commitment of land soldiers it cannot spare. So the Rocky Shore persists: acknowledged, documented, officially condemned, and practically tolerated.</p>
              <p>Their community is built from people the system discarded: deserters from Church military conscription, Merchant Guild laborers who stopped being paid, former harbor workers whose contracts were terminated when automation made them redundant, and a small number of experiment survivors who found the open water more hospitable than any place with walls. They are not unified by ideology but by the shared experience of having been told they did not belong somewhere and deciding that was fine.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Church Authority:</strong> Structurally hostile. They have no stake in the Church's survival and a modest financial interest in its disruption — Church supply ships running late or not at all are good for Rocky Shore prices. They will trade with anyone the Church disapproves of, often at favorable rates.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to the World:</strong> The Harbor Captain who ferries characters to Cultist Island operates on the edge of Rocky Shore territory. His "full fare up front" policy reflects the Shore's foundational principle: debts owed to people the Church considers criminals have no institutional backing. Everything is settled before departure.</p>
              <p><strong>Gameplay Role:</strong> Optional faction encountered near Port City harbor. Provide passage to secondary locations. May offer rare coastal items and information about Cultist Island not available through official channels. Their relationship with the player character shifts based on whether Church insignia is displayed.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="unknown" data-search="formless one enlightenment mystery entity caligo unknown presence ancient">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://static.vecteezy.com/system/resources/thumbnails/053/964/333/small/a-vibrant-silhouette-of-a-head-with-a-bright-sun-at-the-center-radiating-rays-symbolizing-enlightenment-and-creativity-png.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Formless One of Enlightenment">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Beyond Classification</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Ancient Presence · Formless Entity</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Formless One of Enlightenment</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"It has no shape because shape is a limitation it chose not to accept."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unclassified</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Pre-Lumen</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Pre-Caligo</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p><strong>Nature:</strong> Unclassified. Predates all existing religious and faction frameworks in the world of Elucidate. Neither Caligo nor Lumen; neither enemy nor ally in any documented sense.</p>
              <p><strong>Origin:</strong> Unknown. References to an entity matching this description exist in pre-Church texts that have largely been destroyed or suppressed. What survives is fragmentary and resistant to consistent interpretation.</p>
              <p><strong>Appearance:</strong> Formless. Witnesses who claim to have perceived it describe the experience as one of comprehension rather than vision — a sense of something understood rather than something seen. Physical descriptions are absent from all accounts.</p>
              <p><strong>Relationship to Caligo:</strong> ??? — some recovered texts suggest the Formless One and Caligo are distinct phenomena that have been historically conflated. Others suggest a relationship that the available language cannot accurately describe. No conclusion has been reached.</p>
              <p><strong>Relationship to Lumen:</strong> The Church of Lumen does not acknowledge its existence. Whether this is institutional suppression or genuine institutional ignorance is unknown.</p>
              <p><strong>Effect on Mind State:</strong> Encounters with the Formless One of Enlightenment do not increase Mind State in the expected manner. Some recorded cases indicate a temporary reduction. The mechanism is not understood.</p>
              <p><strong>Purpose:</strong> ??? — if it has one, it has not disclosed it through any means that current interpretive frameworks can process.</p>
              <div class="npc-quote">"To be without form is not to be without presence. The absence of shape is not emptiness. It is potential that has refused to commit."<cite>— Fragment, pre-Church text, source unverified</cite></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="zones-of-terrors" data-search="formless god decay rot deterioration unknown entity borderlands unrecorded presence">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://png.pngtree.com/png-vector/20250423/ourmid/pngtree-lonely-winter-tree-silhouette-png-image_16043645.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Formless God of Decay">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">??? · Origin Unknown</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Entity · ??? · Unclassified</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Formless One of Decay</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">Records referencing this presence are incomplete. What remains describes a gradual wrongness — not an arrival, but a revelation that something had already been there.</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">???</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unrecorded Origin</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unaffiliated</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p><strong>Classification:</strong> Unrecorded. Church archivists have noted its presence in fragmented field reports under three different designations, none of which agree on what they describe. The reports share only one consistent detail: organic material in the surrounding area had deteriorated beyond what the elapsed time could account for.</p>
              <p><strong>Nature:</strong> Unknown. Whether this entity is a manifestation of Caligo, a distinct cosmic presence, or something else entirely has not been determined. Investigators dispatched to document it have returned with missing time, corrupted notes, and — in one case — no notes at all. The investigator in that case could not explain what had happened to them.</p>
              <p><strong>Presence:</strong> Said to be tied to rot and the slow deterioration of material things. Not violent in any recorded account. Simply present. The decay continues whether or not it is observed. Whether it causes the decay or merely accompanies it is a distinction no surviving witness has been able to clarify.</p>
              <p><strong>Current Status:</strong> Unverified. Whereabouts unknown. It is unclear if it can be said to be anywhere in the conventional sense.</p>
              <div class="npc-quote">[ Researcher notation, date unrecorded: &ldquo;The wood of the door had deteriorated past the point of usefulness. The stone beneath the door had not. The stone was older. I do not know what this means.&rdquo; ]<cite>— Partial field note, origin unrecorded</cite></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="zones-of-terrors" data-search="formless god plague outbreak mass death borderlands village unverified unrecorded entity unknown">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/58dfd1f7ff7c50123172961d/80544ebd-2f83-4fc2-ae12-9f662141f1c6/rare+disease+banner.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Formless God of Plague">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Borderlands · Unverified</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Entity · ??? · Suspected Cause</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Formless One of Plague</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">Accounts from the Borderlands describe an outbreak of unknown origin. A village. Mass death over the span of eleven days. No confirmed cause. No confirmed presence. The records stop mid-sentence.</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">???</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Borderlands</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unverified</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p><strong>Incident:</strong> Partially recorded. A Church field report, dated approximately three decades before the current campaign, references a plague outbreak in a village near the Borderlands. The outbreak's origin was not identified by the responding unit. The responding unit's follow-up report was never filed.</p>
              <p><strong>Casualties:</strong> Unverified. Survivor accounts, recovered secondhand from traders passing through the region afterward, describe total or near-total death within the settlement over an eleven-day period. The traders did not enter the village. They described the smell from the road and did not stop.</p>
              <p><strong>Attribution:</strong> Conflicting. Some Church internal documents attribute the event to Caligo contamination reaching a critical threshold. Others contain a margin notation — unsigned — that reads only: <em>not Caligo. Something else.</em> The document containing that notation was later marked for archival destruction. It survived by accident.</p>
              <p><strong>Entity Status:</strong> Unconfirmed existence. The designation "Formless God of Plague" does not appear in any official Church record. It appears in one recovered Cult document in a list of entities the Cult itself claims not to understand. No further detail is provided. The list entry ends with a dash and nothing after it.</p>
              <div class="npc-quote">[ Margin notation, origin unsigned: &ldquo;The sickness did not spread the way sickness spreads. It arrived complete.&rdquo; ]<cite>— Church internal document, marked for destruction · Recovered status: incomplete</cite></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="zones-of-terrors" data-search="anthropophagist cannibalism massacre festival icy mountain envoy one three missing unknown formless god manifestation human">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="https://images-wixmp-ed30a86b8c4ca887773594c2.wixmp.com/f/32eb0cb8-99bd-4638-b0ea-c46a1eb6a650/dih8up6-ee778e52-c436-4cb0-815e-064b262c4f68.png/v1/fit/w_414,h_414/katie_killerson_s_mouth_7__bloody___png__by_gavin53zan_dih8up6-414w.png?token=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..5x1Jms5IyjxqAnJgVifY_YiWY9Wz-UN5Bp1Ycx-m5R8" alt="NPC Portrait — The Anthropophagist">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">??? · Icy Mountain Region</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Human Manifestation · ??? · Case File: Incomplete</div>
            <div class="npc-name">The Anthropophagist</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">Documented. Partially. What is known: a figure of human appearance. A village near an icy mountain. A festival that ended before the final hour. Two Envoys dispatched to investigate. Neither returned. The case file was not closed — it was abandoned.</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unknown Origin</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Unaffiliated</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Envoy Case — Open</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p><strong>Classification:</strong> Believed to be a physical human manifestation of an unknown Formless God — a designation the Church has not officially acknowledged and the Cult has not formally categorized. The Anthropophagist presents as human in every external respect. This is considered significant. No further detail on what "manifested as" means in practical terms has been recorded by any surviving investigator.</p>
              <p><strong>The Incident:</strong> A solemn festival held annually by a village near the icy mountain. The festival's purpose is unrecorded — the village's records did not survive. Accounts recovered from nearby settlements describe it as a mourning ritual. Whether the Anthropophagist attended as a participant, arrived uninvited, or had always been present in the village prior to that night is not known. The outcome is: a cannibalistic massacre during the festival's final gathering. Number of victims: unrecorded. Survivors: unconfirmed.</p>
              <p><strong>Investigation — Envoy One:</strong> Dispatched within the week following the first reports. Envoy One reached the village perimeter. Contact was lost. Recovery team dispatched two weeks later found Envoy One's equipment at the treeline. Envoy One was not found.</p>
              <p><strong>Investigation — Envoy Three:</strong> Dispatched as follow-up, six weeks after Envoy One's disappearance. Envoy Three's last filed communication indicated they had made contact with a single individual matching the description of the Anthropophagist in the village outskirts. That communication was filed at the regional Church outpost. No subsequent communications were received. Envoy Three was not found. Their equipment was not found. The regional outpost closed the communication log with the notation: <em>case suspended pending further resources.</em> No further resources were allocated.</p>
              <p><strong>Current Status:</strong> Unknown. Whether the Anthropophagist remains in the mountain region, has moved, or continues to exist in the conventional sense has not been determined. The Church has not publicly acknowledged the incident. The village near the icy mountain does not appear on current Church-issued maps.</p>
              <div class="npc-quote">[ Final logged entry, Envoy Three — partial transmission: &ldquo;It looked at me as if it recognized — &rdquo; ]<cite>— Communication log, Regional Church Outpost · Entry marked incomplete · Case suspended</cite></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>
		
		<article class="npc-card" data-zone="zones-of-terror" data-search="caligo manifestation boss entity apex horror">
		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">
			<img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-elucidate_idle_corrupted1_cultist_npc_down.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Caligo Manifestation">

		  <div class="npc-image-wrap">

        <article class="npc-card" data-zone="home-village" data-search="melted villager home village caligo corruption transformation horror enemy eldritch failed experiment ruins">
          <div class="npc-image-wrap">
            <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-elucidate_idle_melted_male_villager_npc_down.png" alt="NPC Portrait — Melted Villager">
            <div class="npc-zone-tag">Home Village</div>
          </div>
          <div class="npc-body">
            <div class="npc-role">Enemy · Corrupted Civilian</div>
            <div class="npc-name">Melted Villager</div>
            <div class="npc-lore-short">"They do not move like people anymore. They move like people who have forgotten, partway through the motion, what people are for."</div>
            <div class="npc-faction-row">
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Home Village</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Caligo-Corrupted</span>
              <span class="npc-faction-tag">Enemy</span>
            </div>
            <div class="npc-lore-full">
              <p>The Melted Villagers are what remains of the Home Village's population after Caligo's influence passed through unchecked — not killed, not transformed into something new, but arrested partway through both. They retain the shape of people: two arms, upright posture, faces oriented forward. The shape is where the resemblance ends. Their proportions have shifted imperceptibly but wrongly, joints bending in directions that should be impossible, skin carrying the texture of something that was once solid and is no longer certain it wants to be. They are the village's history made literal — a community that was used up and left behind, still present, still moving, no longer able to articulate why.</p>
              <p>What makes them psychologically distinct from standard Eldritch Horrors is their origin. They were not created by Caligo intentionally. They are the byproduct of proximity — civilians caught within sustained Caligo exposure without the mental conditioning the Church spent years developing in its subjects. The Church built Ignis specifically to survive what the Melted Villagers could not. Standing in the ruins of the Home Village, encountering them, this context is not stated. It does not need to be. The player arrives at the implication themselves, and the implication is worse for not being explained.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to Caligo:</strong> The Melted Villagers represent Caligo's effect on the unprepared mind and body — not evil, not intentional, simply the consequence of extended contact with something that does not register human continuity as meaningful. The corruption is not a punishment. It is indifference made physical.</p>
              <p><strong>Connection to the Church's Program:</strong> The Home Village's proximity to the original orphanage was not accidental. Church records in the Hidden Basement reference the village as a "passive observation zone" — its population's deterioration was documented over years as control-group data. The Melted Villagers are, in this light, the experiment's uncredited subjects: the ones who were neither saved nor expelled, simply left in place to demonstrate what the enhancement program was designed to prevent.</p>
              <p><strong>Gameplay Behavior:</strong> Slow, persistent pursuit. They do not retreat. They do not flinch at knockback the way standard enemies do — the Blunderbuss staggers them but does not stop them. Their threat is attrition: they keep coming, and every encounter in the Home Village accelerates MS at the doubled rate. The correct response is to avoid where possible. Fighting all of them is a trap. MS +8 per encounter. Drop: raw materials, occasional Ink Vial.</p>
              <div class="npc-quote">"The headstones have no names on them. Someone buried these people. And then something that was almost these people stood up again."<cite>— Mercenary (Ignis), internal monologue, Home Village entry</cite></div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </article>

<script>

const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      entry.target.classList.add('revealed');
      observer.unobserve(entry.target);
    }
  });
}, { threshold: 0.15, rootMargin: '0px' });

document.querySelectorAll('.npc-card').forEach((el, i) => {
  const delay = Math.min(i * 0.02, 0.2);
  el.style.transition = `
    opacity 0.3s ${delay}s ease,
    transform 0.3s ${delay}s ease,
    border-color 0.2s,
    box-shadow 0.2s
  `;
  observer.observe(el);
});

document.querySelectorAll('.filter-btn').forEach(btn => {
  btn.addEventListener('click', function() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.filter-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
    this.classList.add('active');
    const filter = this.dataset.filter;
    let visibleCount = 0;
    document.querySelectorAll('.npc-card').forEach(card => {
      const zone = card.dataset.zone || '';
      if (filter === 'all' || zone === filter) {
        card.style.display = '';
        visibleCount++;
      } else {
        card.style.display = 'none';
      }
    });
    document.getElementById('no-results').classList.toggle('visible', visibleCount === 0);
  });
});

const inputEl = document.getElementById('search-input');
const overlayEl = document.getElementById('search-results-overlay');

const searchData = [
  { title: 'Blacksmith Conrad', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Walled City armorer who reads soldiers. Knows which ones come back and which ones don\'t.' },
  { title: 'Draft Officer', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Issues the Mercenary\'s mission. Honest about knowing nothing above his pay grade.' },
  { title: 'Librarian Scholar', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Gives the real history of Caligo quietly, then walks away as if he said nothing remarkable.' },
  { title: 'Confession Booth Witnesses', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Civilians whispering the human cost of the Church\'s experiments in the dark.' },
  { title: 'Tavern Keeper — The Gilded Anchor', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Twenty years of harbor intelligence. Reads storms. Reads Konrad. Offers the second ale for free.' },
  { title: 'Harbor Captain', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Will take passage to Cultist Island. Full fare up front. Three of seven ships came back with fewer crew.' },
  { title: 'Guild Master', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Warns Konrad against investigation. Knows what he knows. Has made different calculations about the same truth.' },
  { title: 'Tribe Elder', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Twelve fighters left. Notices Ignis stopped at the watchtower. Trusts him based on sixty seconds.' },
  { title: 'Tribe Chief', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Gives the Wind Essence. A promise, not a gift. Bring her back when it is done, if you can.' },
  { title: 'Cult Priest', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Ignis listened for four minutes before entering the island. That listening is the beginning of everything.' },
  { title: 'Imprisoned Experiment Subject', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Father Calum knew their name. The way a manufacturer knows a production number.' },
  { title: 'Church Attendant', section: 'NPCs', excerpt: 'Reports adverse reactions. Files the paperwork. Between the harm and the authority with insufficient leverage at either end.' },
];

function escapeHtml(str) {
  return str.replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/"/g,'&quot;');
}
function highlightMatch(text, q) {
  const idx = text.toLowerCase().indexOf(q);
  if (idx < 0) return escapeHtml(text);
  return escapeHtml(text.slice(0,idx)) + '<mark class="search-highlight">' + escapeHtml(text.slice(idx, idx+q.length)) + '</mark>' + escapeHtml(text.slice(idx+q.length));
}

function performSearch() {
  const q = inputEl.value.trim().toLowerCase();
  overlayEl.innerHTML = '';

  let visibleCount = 0;
  document.querySelectorAll('.npc-card').forEach(card => {
    const searchStr = (card.dataset.search || '') + ' ' + (card.querySelector('.npc-name')?.textContent || '');
    const activeFilter = document.querySelector('.filter-btn.active')?.dataset.filter || 'all';
    const zone = card.dataset.zone || '';
    const zoneMatch = activeFilter === 'all' || zone === activeFilter;
    const textMatch = !q || q.length < 2 || searchStr.toLowerCase().includes(q);

    if (zoneMatch && textMatch) {
      card.style.display = '';
      visibleCount++;
    } else {
      card.style.display = 'none';
    }
  });

  document.getElementById('no-results').classList.toggle('visible', visibleCount === 0 && q.length >= 2);

  if (!q || q.length < 2) { overlayEl.classList.remove('visible'); return; }

  const results = searchData.filter(item =>
    item.title.toLowerCase().includes(q) || item.excerpt.toLowerCase().includes(q)
  ).slice(0, 7);

  if (!results.length) {
    overlayEl.innerHTML = '<div class="search-no-result">No results for "' + escapeHtml(q) + '"</div>';
  } else {
    results.forEach(item => {
      const div = document.createElement('div');
      div.className = 'search-result-item';
      div.innerHTML = `<div class="sr-title">${highlightMatch(item.title, q)} <span style="font-size:9px;color:var(--text-dim);margin-left:6px;">${item.section}</span></div><div class="sr-excerpt">${highlightMatch(item.excerpt.slice(0,80)+'…', q)}</div>`;
      div.onclick = () => { overlayEl.classList.remove('visible'); };
      overlayEl.appendChild(div);
    });
  }
  overlayEl.classList.add('visible');
}

inputEl.addEventListener('input', performSearch);
inputEl.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Escape') { overlayEl.classList.remove('visible'); inputEl.blur(); } });
document.addEventListener('click', e => {
  if (!e.target.closest('.search-wrap') && !e.target.closest('#search-results-overlay')) {
    overlayEl.classList.remove('visible');
  }
});
</script>
</body>
</html>

  color: var(--gold-pale); letter-spacing: 1px; margin-bottom: 4px;
}

  font-family: 'Cinzel', serif; font-size: 8px;

}

    <section class="page-hero">
      <div class="hero-bg-stars" id="stars-container"></div>
      <div class="page-hero-tag">Elucidate RPG DLC · Items Archive</div>
      <h1 class="page-hero-title">Items, <span class="accent">Artifacts</span> &amp; Relics</h1>
      <div class="hero-line"></div>
      <p class="page-hero-sub">Every object in Elucidate carries the weight of its origin, forged, fractured, or written into existence by the same hands that shaped the war between Lumen and Caligo.</p>
    </section>

    <div class="filter-section">
      <div class="filter-bar">
        <span class="filter-label">Filter :</span>

        <span id="item-count"></span>

    <!-- ══ FRAGMENTS OF CALIGO ══ -->
    <div class="section-divider" style="padding: 32px 60px 0;"><div class="div-line"></div><div class="div-icon">◈</div><div class="div-line"></div></div>
    <section class="special-section" id="fragments">
      <div class="section-header">
        <span class="section-tag">Sacred · Dangerous · Contested</span>
        <h2 class="section-title">Fragments of <span class="caligo">Caligo</span></h2>
        <p class="section-desc">Physical manifestations of the Formless One's influence. The Church wants them contained. The Cult wants them worshipped. The truth is worse than either.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="fragment-focus">
        <div class="fragment-intro">
          <p>Three Fragments exist in the known world. Each is a crystallized shard of <strong>Caligo's presence</strong>, not a piece of the entity itself, but an echo of the void it leaves behind wherever it passes. The Church calls them <strong>sacred enemy relics</strong>. The Cult calls them <strong>holy instruments</strong>. Both descriptions are wrong in the same way.</p>
        </div>
        <div class="fragment-grid" id="fragment-entries">

          <!-- LARGEST FRAGMENT -->
          <div class="fragment-card" data-category="fragment">
            <div class="fragment-card-img">
              <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Caligo-Fragemnt-1.png" alt="Largest Fragment of Caligo">
            </div>
            <div class="fragment-card-body">
              <div class="fragment-card-name">Largest Fragment of Caligo</div>
              <div class="fragment-card-sub">Eye Form · Fragment · Legendary</div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Function</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">Dropped by the Cult Leader upon defeat. Completing the trio of Fragments is the central objective of the Mercenary's mission. Possessing it triggers the final decision node: deliver to the Church, or withhold.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Description</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">A dense, asymmetrical shard roughly the size of a fist, dark as obsidian but veined with light that does not come from any external source. The surface is warm, not uncomfortably so, but insistently. Looking directly at it for too long produces a sensation described by every carrier as <em>recognition</em>.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Lore</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text lore">The Cult of Caligo has held this Fragment since the Church expelled its founding members. It was not stolen, it was carried out by a child who simply refused to leave it behind. The Cult built their entire theology around it: the Eye Form, they call it, the gaze of the Formless One rendered solid. The Church calls it a corruption node. The Librarian Scholar called it a mirror. He did not explain what it reflects.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-notes">⚠ MS +12 on acquisition. At MS > 75, carriers report hearing a voice that uses their original name, the one before the Church renamed them.</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- FLAME FRAGMENT -->
          <div class="fragment-card" data-category="fragment">
            <div class="fragment-card-img">
              <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Caligo-Fragemnt-2.png" alt="Flame Fragment of Caligo">
            </div>
            <div class="fragment-card-body">
              <div class="fragment-card-name">Flame Fragment of Caligo</div>
              <div class="fragment-card-sub">Fire Form · Fragment · Legendary</div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Function</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">Found in the Tribe Area. Voluntarily surrendered by Pluvia, the Shaman, who has kept it protected from both the Church and Cult. Holding it in combat grants a passive aura that causes hesitation in Eldritch Horrors, they pause for 1.5 seconds before engaging. This advantage vanishes above MS 70.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Description</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">Translucent, amber-hued, shaped like a flame frozen mid-flicker. Unlike other Fragments, this one flickers visibly, a slow, rhythmic pulse that matches no known combustion pattern. It does not burn the holder's skin, but nearby parchment browns at the edges if left adjacent overnight.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Lore</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text lore">The Tribe's elders have passed the Flame Fragment down through seventeen generations without ever fully understanding what they were guarding. Their oral tradition calls it <em>the memory that refuses to cool</em>. Pluvia's Foresight has told her, three times and in three separate visions, that if this Fragment reaches the Church, something ends that cannot be started again. She gives it away anyway. The visions showed that too.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-notes">⚠ The Tribe Elder's last words before the Shaman departs: "Bring her back when it is done. If you can." The qualifier is not accidental.</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- VOID FRAGMENT -->
          <div class="fragment-card" data-category="fragment">
            <div class="fragment-card-img">
              <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Caligo-Fragemnt-3.png" alt="Void Fragment of Caligo">
            </div>
            <div class="fragment-card-body">
              <div class="fragment-card-name">Void Fragment of Caligo</div>
              <div class="fragment-card-sub">Shadow Form · Fragment · Legendary</div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Function</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">Located along the path toward Cultist Island from the Port City.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Description</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text">Perfectly spherical, matte black, approximately the weight of a stone twice its size. No visible seam, no texture, no temperature. It absorbs ambient light in a radius of roughly three feet. Objects placed next to it cast no shadow because the Fragment absorbs shadow itself.</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-section">
                <div class="fragment-section-label">Lore</div>
                <div class="fragment-section-text lore">The Church's mission briefing does not mention this Fragment. It exists in their records under an administrative category titled <em>Indirect Artifacts — Non-Priority Recovery</em>. The record was amended fourteen years ago. Before the amendment, it was listed as <em>Priority One — Do Not Approach Unaccompanied</em>. The reason for the amendment is redacted. Father Calum's personal archive contains a single note: "The Void Fragment does not corrupt. It clarifies. That is the problem."</div>
              </div>
              <div class="fragment-notes">⚠ Unique: grants Caligo Perception passive. Trade-off — the world rendered clearly at all MS levels is not always preferable to the distorted version.</div>
            </div>
          </div>

        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ══ ENLIGHTENMENT ITEMS ══ -->
    <div class="section-divider"><div class="div-line"></div><div class="div-icon">✦</div><div class="div-line"></div></div>
    <section class="enlightenment-section" id="enlightenment">
      <div class="section-header">
        <span class="section-tag">Knowledge · Crafting · Origin</span>
        <h2 class="section-title">The <span class="gold">Enlightenment</span> Artifacts</h2>
        <p class="section-desc">Items born from the Formless One of Enlightenment — knowledge made tangible, or knowledge weaponized.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="enlightenment-focus">

        <!-- BOOK OF ENLIGHTENMENT -->
        <div class="enlightenment-card" data-category="book">
          <div class="enlightenment-card-header">
            <div class="enlightenment-card-img">
              <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_save_book_of_enlightenment.png" alt="Book of Enlightenment">
            </div>
            <div class="enlightenment-card-meta">
              <div class="enlightenment-card-name">Book of Enlightenment</div>
              <div class="enlightenment-card-type">Special Book · Artifact · Legendary</div>
              <div class="crafting-recipe">
                <span class="crafting-label">Crafted from</span>
                <span class="crafting-item">Ancient Paper</span>
                <span class="crafting-arrow">→</span>
                <span class="crafting-item">Book of Enlightenment</span>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="enlightenment-card-body">
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Function / Effect</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text">The Book of Enlightenment carries the <code>save</code> effect, the only item in the game capable of preserving a character's current state.</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Description</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text">Bound in material that resembles gold leaf but is not, closer to compressed light, if that were a material. The pages resist tearing. The text within is not static: it changes between readings, reflecting the reader's current knowledge state back at them with additions the reader did not put there. No two characters read the same book. The words are always legible. The meaning is always just beyond comfortable comprehension.</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text lore">The Formless One of Enlightenment is never named in the game's dialogue, only implied. Where Caligo is described as existing <em>the way weather exists</em>, Enlightenment is its inverse: a presence that does not corrupt but illuminates, and not always kindly. The Book is the only confirmed physical artifact of Enlightenment's influence. It was not created by scholars or the Church. The Ancient Paper from which it is crafted predates the Theocratic capital by four hundred years. The Librarian Scholar, in his extended dialogue, says only: <em>"There are things that want to be known. The book is one of them."</em> He does not explain further. He never explains further. That is the whole of what Enlightenment gives: the information, without the comfort of understanding it.</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Notes</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text">The Book of Enlightenment is the game's rarest crafted item. Its relationship to Caligo is oppositional but not adversarial, Caligo corrupts through absence of meaning; Enlightenment overwhelms through excess of it. Both result in the same risk: a mind that cannot hold what it has been given.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ANCIENT PAPER -->
        <div class="enlightenment-card" data-category="material">
          <div class="enlightenment-card-header">
            <div class="enlightenment-card-img">
              <img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_sheet_ancient_paper.png" alt="Ancient Paper">
            </div>
            <div class="enlightenment-card-meta">
              <div class="enlightenment-card-name">Ancient Paper</div>
              <div class="enlightenment-card-type">Material · Crafting Component · Rare</div>
              <div class="crafting-recipe">
                <span class="crafting-label">Used in</span>
                <span class="crafting-item">Book of Enlightenment</span>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="enlightenment-card-body">
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Function / Effect</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text">Primary crafting material for the Book of Enlightenment. Cannot be used in any other recipe. Found in the Records Archive of the Theocratic Capital, the document references "dual-purpose acquisition" of the Fragments, noting that the Cult's ritual use and the Church's containment protocols share more methodology than either party admits. This is the first evidence, available to any character who finds it, that the Church and Cult are two expressions of the same underlying design.</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Description</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text">Yellowed but intact despite its age. The text upon it is in three scripts, two of which no character in the game can read. The third is administrative Church Latin, and it is the clearest and most damning of the three. The paper does not age further once found, it simply holds its condition indefinitely, the way certain truths resist decay no matter how long they are suppressed.</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-section-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="enlightenment-text lore">The Formless One of Enlightenment did not create the Ancient Paper directly. What Enlightenment did was ensure that whoever needed to find it would find it, that the document would survive every fire, flood, and deliberate destruction the Church attempted. There are singed corners. There are water stains. There is a circular burn mark consistent with something very hot being placed on it and then removed. The text beneath the burn mark is perfectly legible. The Formless One of Enlightenment is patient in a way that has nothing to do with virtue and everything to do with certainty.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

      </div>
    </section>

    <!-- ══ ALL ITEMS ARCHIVE ══ -->
    <div class="section-divider"><div class="div-line"></div><div class="div-icon">◆</div><div class="div-line"></div></div>
    <section class="archive-section" id="all-items">
      <div class="section-header">
        <span class="section-tag">Full Archive</span>
        <h2 class="section-title">Complete <span class="gold">Item Registry</span></h2>
        <p class="section-desc">All weapons, armour, books, materials, potions and accessories catalogued from across the world of Elucidate.</p>
      </div>

      <!-- WEAPONS -->
      <div class="category-group" id="weapons">
        <div class="category-label">
          <span class="category-label-text">⚔ Weapons</span>
          <div class="category-label-line"></div>
        </div>
        <div class="item-cards-grid">

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_blunderbuss.png" alt="Blunderbuss"><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Blunderbuss</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>55</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">The Mercenary's signature starting weapon. Two shots before reload, significant knockback on hit, essential for crowd control in multi-enemy scenarios and for breaking shield formations. Upgradeable to Reinforced Blunderbuss with Iron Ingot ×2 + Silver Wire.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A flared brass muzzle that looks more like a trumpet than a firearm. Heavy, temperamental in moisture, but reliable when maintained. Blacksmith Conrad's words: <em>She has never once let a man down who treated her right.</em></div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Issued by the Church's Third Regiment armory. The chest it came from is marked "Returned by nobody. As usual." Every soldier the Church sends through that armory takes a Blunderbuss. Most do not come back to return it. The ones who do come back changed.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_2handed_makeshift_spear.png" alt="Makeshift Spear"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Makeshift Spear</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">2-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>38</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Reach</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Long reach — keeps distance from Eldritch Horrors. Can be thrown once per combat encounter to lodge in an enemy and briefly immobilize them. Upgradeable to Iron Spear at any blacksmith. Becomes a liability in enclosed spaces like the Underground Laboratory.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A knife strapped to a long stick with leather cord. The improvised nature is visible and irrelevant, the reach is real, and reach is what matters when what is coming toward you should not come close.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Craftable at Conrad's workshop after the introductory dialogue. The worn journal found in the back room of that workshop belongs to a soldier who also carried this spear and was sent to the Home Village. His last entry says something was wrong there for years. He was correct.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_scimitar.png" alt="Scimitar"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Scimitar</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>36</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Curved Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Dropped by Cultist Soldiers. Single-edged, high damage for a one-handed weapon, best used where the Spear's reach is a disadvantage. Favored by the Cult for its cultural resonance, curved blades are associated in Cult theology with the arc of Caligo's influence through time.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A wicked, sweeping curve of steel maintained with unusual care given the carriers. Cultist Soldiers keep their Scimitars ceremonially clean even when everything else they own is weathered. The edge is consistent proof of dedication.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Cult adopted curved blades in the early years of their formation, a deliberate rejection of the Church's straight-sword iconography. What began as symbolism became tradition. The Scimitar the Mercenary might carry after Cultist Island was sharpened by someone who believed in something. That fact does not change what was done with it.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_flintlock.png" alt="Flintlock"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Flintlock</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>46</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">The Merchant's favored weapon. Faster to ready than the Blunderbuss, lower knockback. Best used for precision elimination rather than crowd suppression . Konrad's combat philosophy in one firearm.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Compact, well-balanced, with wood grain smoothed by years of handling. The barrel has been modified, a small modification near the trigger guard that no standard gunsmith would recognize but that reduces misfires in damp conditions. Someone who uses this weapon has thought carefully about not dying.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Port City is a merchant city, and merchants in Port City carry Flintlocks not because they expect violence but because they have learned that expecting violence is irrelevant to whether it arrives. Konrad has carried his for fourteen years. He has fired it twice in that time. Both times were necessary. He does not enjoy the memory of either.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_short_sword.png" alt="Short Sword"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Short Sword</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>28</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Purchasable from the Supply Merchant. Faster attack speed than the Spear. Best in enclosed spaces — corridors, the Underground Laboratory, the Cathedral's inner halls — where a longer weapon's reach becomes a hindrance against walls.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A double-edged steel blade with a simple crossguard. Nothing ceremonial. Nothing wasted. The kind of weapon that was designed for the specific purpose of ending things quickly without announcement.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Standard issue for Church operatives working in close quarters. The Underground Laboratory encounters, where corridors were built to accommodate medical procedure, not combat, were the context for its design. The Church built the corridors narrow deliberately. They did not want two people to be able to pass side by side. Accountability requires witnesses.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_2handed_claymore.png" alt="Claymore"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Claymore</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">2-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>47</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Greatsword</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">High-damage two-hander. The distinctive crossguard ending in quatrefoils is Church iconography, carrying it signals allegiance, or at minimum past allegiance, in ways that affect NPC dialogue in certain zones.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Signifies both faith and a very long reach. The quatrefoils were added as a design choice three generations ago by a Church armorer who believed weapons should carry the marks of their purpose. The purpose was violence in service of doctrine. The ornamentation does not change that, but it makes it easier to perform.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Holy Knights carry Claymores. When the Mercenary fights former Church allies in Ending B, the weight of that specific weapon type in his inventory, if he is carrying one, triggers a unique internal monologue: recognition without sentimentality, a distinction the game insists upon. He has moved from using the weapon to carrying the memory of using it. That is different.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>
		  
		            <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_cleaver.png" alt="Cleaver"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Cleaver</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>33</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Heavy Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">A wide, heavy swing weapon with significant stagger on hit. Particularly effective against lightly armored enemies and works well in close-quarters environments. The rectangular profile prevents it from being used in tight stabbing motions, a limitation that rewards positioning over finesse.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A massive, rectangular slab of cast iron with a single purpose.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Originally a butcher's tool from the Port City market district. Its previous owner never had cause to use it as a weapon. The person who found it after the Port City riots did. The cleaver does not know the difference between a carcass and a cause, and neither did the war that followed.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_dagger.png" alt="Dagger">
			<span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Dagger</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>25</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Short Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Fast attack speed with minimal telegraph. Ideal for situations requiring quick successive strikes. Concealable in inventory slots that larger weapons cannot occupy. Pairs with most one-handed off-hand options. The low damage per swing is offset by the speed at which additional swings can follow.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A simple dagger.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The simplicity is the point. Everything in Elucidate carries history, the Dagger carries the honest history of things that are made to do exactly one thing and do it without ceremony. The Church distributed them to low-rank initiates who could not be trusted with a longsword yet. Most never needed to use them. A few did.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_2handed_longsword.png" alt="Longsword"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Longsword</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">2-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>32</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Balanced attack profile with solid damage and adequate swing speed. The reliable choice for players seeking consistent one-handed performance without committing to heavier weapons. Compatible with shield use and effective across most enemy types encountered in the Theocratic Capital and surrounding zones.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A straight, double-edged blade made out of cold-forged steel.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Cold-forging was banned by the Church's armory standard forty years ago, the process is slower and more expensive than smelting. The longswords still in circulation that were made this way are relics of a pre-Church manufacturing tradition. The blade remembers what the institution forgot about taking time to make something properly.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_iron_axe.png" alt="Iron Axe"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Iron Axe</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>30</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Axe</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Moderate damage with high penetration against layered armor. The axe head's geometry transfers force differently than a blade, useful against Holy Knights in plate configurations where edge weapons lose efficiency. Found commonly in village settlements and as a drop from Forest Patrol enemies.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A tool used to cut wood but it does not mean that it can't cut a human.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Home Village used iron axes for the lumber work that supplied Church construction. When the village was abandoned, the axes remained. Someone, later, found them. The description is written in the inventory as a statement of fact without emotion, which is its own kind of emotion.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_corsairs_saber.png" alt="Corsair's Saber"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Corsair's Saber</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
