              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>32</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Curved Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Curved geometry offers superior slashing arcs compared to straight blades at the same damage tier. Found in Port City's harbor district and as a drop from coastal traders who have been pushed to desperation. The salt-resistance treatment on the blade means it does not degrade in moisture-heavy zones like the coastal passages.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A curved, single-edged blade forged to resist the salt spray of the seas.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Port City's corsair trade collapsed when the Church imposed maritime restrictions. The blades remain, confiscated, redistributed, occasionally lost, making their way inland through channels that no longer exist. The saber carries the memory of open water even in landlocked rooms. Konrad recognizes the make. He does not say how.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_knife.png" alt="Knife"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Knife</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>22</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Short Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Lowest damage among bladed one-handed weapons but retains all attack-speed advantages of the short-blade class. Useful as a secondary fallback when a primary weapon's durability is low. Throwable as a ranged attack for a single use before reverting to melee only, a mechanic shared with no other non-spear weapon.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A single-edged steel blade.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Every cook, farmer, and tradesperson in the Elucidate world carries one. The knife does not ask to be a weapon. It is the most honest object in the game, designed for a purpose, repurposed by circumstance. The description is three words. Three words is all it needs.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_improvised_shiv.png" alt="Improvised Shiv"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Improvised Shiv</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>23</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Improvised</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Lowest-tier crafted weapon. Produced when no standard blade is available, requires only Leather Scraps and a Stick. Irregular edges cause a bleed-chance proc that standard knives do not. Its improvised nature means it cannot be upgraded at any blacksmith, but it bridges inventory gaps in early-game resource-scarce runs.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A jagged piece of rebar wrapped in leather.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Underground Laboratory has rebar. The rebar has jagged ends where the construction stopped mid-process and was never resumed. The leather wrapping was added by someone who intended to survive long enough to need a comfortable grip. Whoever they were, they left the shiv behind. The fact that it was left and not taken says something. No character in the game says what.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_steel_hammer.png" alt="Steel Hammer"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Steel Hammer</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>25</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Blunt</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Mid-tier blunt weapon. Toothed head increases trauma on armored foes, the serrations catch on plate seams and transfer additional force into the joint rather than glancing off the surface. Found in Conrad's workshop as a secondary item and dropped by Church construction crews encountered in the Theocratic Capital environs.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A haft topped with a heavy, toothed metal head.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Conrad keeps three of them in his workshop. He does not explain why three. When asked, he says the first one is for work, the second is for emergencies, and the third is for if the emergency does not go well. He says it without smiling. He is not joking.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_stiletto.png" alt="Stiletto"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Stiletto</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>29</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Piercing</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">High penetration against plated armor, the triangular or square cross-section wedges through gaps in plate that flat blades cannot enter. Dropped exclusively by Church Assassins, which ensures it appears only in mid-to-late game encounters. Not upgradeable through standard blacksmith routes, its specialized geometry requires a different materials path.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A long, slender spike of triangular or square-sectioned steel.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Church Assassins carry two. The first is for the assignment. The second is for if something went wrong with the first. Their operational doctrine requires that every engagement end without testimony from the subject. The Stiletto's geometry is designed for exactly this, it leaves evidence of puncture but not of identity. The Church has spent considerable effort ensuring that the things it does cannot be named by what was left behind.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_dirk.png" alt="Dirk"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Dirk</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">1-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>26</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Long Blade</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Longer blade than the Dagger or Knife. Reaches further while maintaining single-handed use. The heavy grip shifts balance toward the hand, reducing wrist fatigue in extended combat encounters. Favored by players who want the speed of a short weapon with slightly more reach than the standard short-blade class provides.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A long, straight blade with a single sharp edge and a heavy grip.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The dirk was the personal sidearm of Church officers before the rank was abolished and the role folded into the Holy Knight classification. The officers are gone. The dirks remained in the armory, catalogued under a rank that no longer exists, issued to no one in particular and therefore available to anyone who knew where to look.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>


          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_2handed_maul.png" alt="Maul"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Maul</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">2-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>43</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Two-Handed Blunt</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Matched damage to the Spear but with a dramatically different attack profile, short range, massive stagger, effective against shielded enemies where the blunt mass bypasses shield edge and breaks stance. The slowest swing arc in the two-handed category. Each swing is a commitment. Missing with the Maul has consequences.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A massive, six-sided block of reinforced iron welded to an ash-wood pole.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Six sides is deliberate, each face of the head is a different weight distribution, meaning no two strikes land quite the same way. This was an engineering choice made by the Blacksmith's Guild before the Guild was absorbed into the Church's production mandate. The Maul is one of the last objects they made on their own terms. The ash-wood pole is original. It has not been replaced.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_2handed_spear.png" alt="Spear"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Spear</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">2-Handed</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>43</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Reach</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Upgraded successor to the Makeshift Spear. Retains the long-reach advantage and one throwable use per combat encounter, the thrown Spear now lodges and deals tick damage rather than simple immobilization. Properly forged, unlike its Makeshift counterpart, meaning it can be upgraded at Conrad's workshop for further stat increases.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Seven feet of seasoned ash tipped with a leaf-shaped steel head.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Distinct from the Makeshift Spear in every way that matters, where the Makeshift is improvised survival, the Spear is craft. Seasoned ash means the wood was cut and dried for two years before being shaped. Leaf-shaped heads are an older design, pre-Church, from a military tradition the Church does not acknowledge in its doctrine. The weapon remembers a history it was not permitted to have.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>
		  
          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_short_bow.png" alt="Short Bow"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Short Bow</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>27</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Entry-level ranged weapon. Fastest draw speed among bows, essential for the Marksmanship and Rapid Fire skill chains. Small frame allows use in enclosed areas where the Longbow would catch walls and ceilings. Effective in Zones of Terrors where maintaining distance is more valuable than maximizing damage output per shot.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A recurved frame small enough to be slung over a shoulder without catching on low-hanging branches.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Tribe used Short Bows for two purposes — hunting and ceremony — and considered them the same act approached from different directions. Pluvia's early combat options reflect this: the bow was not her first weapon. It was her first tool. The distinction, in Tribe practice, matters significantly.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_longbow.png" alt="Longbow"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Longbow</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>29</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Greater effective range than the Short Bow with improved damage per arrow. Draw time is longer, the Rapid Fire skill book mitigates this significantly. Best used in open environments like the Plains zones and Coastal Cliffs where the additional range pays off. Awkward in the Underground Laboratory's tight corridors.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A simple weapon, yet it transformed the battlefields of the world.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The description is unusually historical for an item entry. No character in the game identifies who wrote the inventory text, it reads like an excerpt from a document that predates the Theocratic Capital's founding. The Church suppressed historical military texts to prevent soldiers from learning that the battles they were told never happened did, in fact, happen. The Longbow knows better.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_heavy_crossbow.png" alt="Heavy Crossbow"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Heavy Crossbow</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>26</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Lower raw damage than the bows but the bolt travels faster and with a flatter trajectory, more reliable over distance against moving targets. Reload time is the longest of any ranged weapon. Ideal for players who prefer to guarantee a hit over taking multiple fast shots. The mechanical draw means Strength modifiers from heavy armor builds apply to bolt velocity.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A compact yet deadly ranged weapon that trades speed for devastating precision.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Church's siege division used Heavy Crossbows for garrison defense, positions where reload time was less critical than shot certainty. When the siege division was disbanded, the crossbows were supposed to be destroyed. The armory records show thirty-seven destroyed. The armory originally held forty-two. The five unaccounted for appear in the game world in places the Church has no record of putting them.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="weapon">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_longrange_musket.png" alt="Musket"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Musket</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-weapon">Ranged</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>50</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Longranged</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Highest ranged damage among standard weapons, surpassed only by the Blunderbuss at close range. Single shot before a lengthy reload. The long barrel provides maximum accuracy at the cost of usability in tight spaces. Best deployed as an opener for high-value single targets before switching to a secondary weapon. The Marksmanship passive skill book directly enhances Musket performance.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A long-barreled firearm built for battlefield volleys and disciplined marksmen.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Church's Third Regiment trained with Muskets in formation, volleys from disciplined ranks rather than individual marksmanship. The regiment was deployed once, at the Coastal Settlement incident, and the records of what they fired at have been removed from the Archives. The Muskets were returned cleaned. The people who cleaned them did not file reports.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>


        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- ARMOUR -->
      <div class="category-group" id="armour">
        <div class="category-label">
          <span class="category-label-text">🛡 Armour</span>
          <div class="category-label-line"></div>
        </div>
        <div class="item-cards-grid">

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_priests_robe.png" alt="Priest's Robe"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Priest's Robe</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>8</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Mind Res <span>+1</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Dropped by Father Calum upon defeat in Ending B. Low physical defense, but the Mind Resistance bonus reflects what wearing the Church's authority does to a person's psychological armor, even stripped from the man who abused it. Equipping it triggers a unique reaction from any surviving NPCs who recognize it.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">White, or once white, now off-white in the way that long-held certainty becomes off-white when it is finally examined. Embroidered with the Church seal at the hem. The stitching is immaculate. Someone who cared about this robe cared about it deeply before the caring became the mechanism of something terrible.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Father Calum was dressed as C-1 before he was dressed as a priest. The robe was given to him when the Church decided that the most successful product of their program should be the program's administrator. He has worn it every day for thirty years. His personal journal's last entry ends mid-sentence. The robe outlasts the sentence.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_dark_priests_robe.png" alt="Dark Priest's Robe"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Dark Priest's Robe</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>8</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Speed <span>+1</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Dropped by Cultist higher-ups. Trades Mind Resistance for Speed, the Cult's philosophy in a stat line. They move faster because they stopped trying to hold their psychological ground and started moving with the current. Whether that is wisdom or collapse is the game's central question.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Black, well-maintained despite the conditions of Cultist Island. The embroidery is Caligo's sigil, not the stylized version used in Cult propaganda, but the original form, which the Cult's founding members transcribed from their earliest Fragment interactions. It looks like a question mark that has lost its curve.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Cult adopted black robes as a direct negation of the Church's white, but over time, the symbolism inverted. White for the Church means purity of purpose; black for the Cult came to mean transparency about what they are: instruments of something that does not care about them, who chose that relationship over the alternative of pretending the caring was real.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>


          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_iron_plate.png" alt="Plate Armour"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Body Plate Armour</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>28</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">High physical protection, no secondary effects. Best in structured combat zones, the Theocratic Capital Ending B, the port landings, where physical damage is predictable and consistent. Slows movement in ways that make it inadvisable in the Zones of Terrors where evasion matters more than absorption.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Made from different alloys, offering moderate to high protection. The craftsmanship is undeniably skilled. The Church funds good armor because dead soldiers are expensive; the investment is purely logistical. The soldiers inside the armor draw whatever comfort they can from that.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Church-issue Plate Armour bears no personal markings, soldiers are not supposed to be individuals inside it. This was a deliberate design decision documented in a Church administrative bulletin titled <em>Standardization of Field Equipment for Identity Consistency</em>. The bulletin is seventeen pages long. The reason it takes seventeen pages to explain why armor should not be personalized is its own kind of lore.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_guard_coif.png" alt="Coif"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Coif</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>7</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Minimal defense, maximum comfort. The Coif's soft linen construction provides negligible physical protection but is the only head slot item that does not affect Mind State readings in the game's passive anxiety system, wearing heavier helmets in psychologically charged zones incrementally increases MS through a "confinement" modifier. The Coif bypasses this entirely.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A white linen bonnet-like cap.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Church initiates wore Coifs during their first year of service, considered a mark of incomplete formation, removed once the initiate received their full kit. The Coif was not designed to protect. It was designed to identify someone who was still becoming what the Church wanted them to be. Several characters in Elucidate are still, in some sense, wearing theirs.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_iron_helmet.png" alt="Iron Helmet"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Iron Helmet</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>20</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Solid mid-to-high tier head protection. Cast iron construction provides reliable defense without the weight penalties of forged plate variants. The standard Church line infantry helmet, well distributed across the mid-game as a drop item, meaning it becomes accessible at a point where its DEF 20 rating represents a meaningful upgrade over leather alternatives.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A helmet made out of cast iron.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Cast from Church foundry iron, the same ore mined in the northern territories. The helmet was made to a specification written forty years ago and has not been revised since. It protects exactly as well as it was designed to protect. The soldiers wearing it have changed. The helmet has not. The Church finds consistency in materials easier to maintain than consistency in people.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/weapon_1handed_cloth_hood.png" alt="Cloth Hood"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Cloth Hood</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>5</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Marginally better than the Coif in physical defense, similar in spirit. Provides the same confinement-modifier exemption in MS-sensitive zones as the Coif, the cloth class of head armour shares this property. Ideal for Mind State management runs where preserving psychological stability is more valuable than incremental defense gains.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A helmet made of cloth.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Calling it a helmet is the inventory system being charitable. It is a hood. It covers the head. It does not protect what it covers. The people in Elucidate who wear cloth hoods do so because they had nothing else, or because they chose comfort over armor, or because something happened to their real helmet and they kept moving anyway. All three histories are valid. All three are present in the game world.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_chainmail_hood.png" alt="Chainmail Hood"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Chainmail Hood</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>13</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Flexible linked-metal construction specifically effective against arrow and bolt impacts, the interlocking rings distribute puncture force across a broader area. Its anti-ranged specialization makes it uniquely valuable in Cultist Island encounters where archers are common. Lower effective defense against blunt weapons compared to its raw DEF number suggests.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A hood made to protect people from arrows.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Cult's archers were trained at a different doctrine than the Church's, aimed at specific targets rather than area coverage. The Chainmail Hood was developed in response to early conflicts where Cult arrows came from unexpected angles. Knowing what someone developed their armor to stop tells you a great deal about what they expected to encounter. The Church expected arrows from people who knew where to aim.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_plate_helmet.png" alt="Plate Helmet"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Plate Helmet</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>22</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">High-defense head armour available in the mid-to-late game. Heavy construction provides the highest physical defense in the head slot outside of the Guard Bascinet. Pairs with Plate Armour and Iron Cuirass for a cohesive heavy-armour build. The enclosed visor reduces peripheral vision, a minor mechanical penalty on dodge-timing checks.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A heavy armour made to protect its wearer.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Plate Helmet is the point at which armor stops being practical and starts being a statement. Something fully encased in plate is telling the world that it expects to be struck and has made peace with that expectation. The Church's elite soldiers wear it not because they fear being hit, they wear it so that whatever strikes them does not finish the sentence it started.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_iron_mask.png" alt="Iron Mask"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Iron Mask</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>12</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Face-coverage only, the Iron Mask leaves the crown and back of the head unprotected. Its DEF 12 applies exclusively to frontal face impacts, making it most effective as a supplementary piece for characters already wearing a hood or coif. Unique among head armour: removes the character's facial expressions from NPC reaction calculations in social encounters.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A mask made out of cast iron.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Iron Mask was used in the Church's detention program, not worn by guards, but fitted on subjects during transport to remove their identity from public record. A masked subject in transit was administratively a package, not a person. The masks were supposed to be destroyed after use. Several were not. They appear in the game world in places that were once transit points. The Church has thorough records of where they should be. Less thorough records of where they are.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_headware_guard_bascinet.png" alt="Guard Bascinet"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Guard Bascinet</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Head</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>25</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Head Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Highest defense in the head slot. The integrated throat and neck guard extends protection into the upper body where standard helmets leave a gap. Dropped exclusively by Elite Guard enemies in the Theocratic Capital's inner sanctum, encountered late-game only. Equipping it locks the character into heavy-armour movement physics for the duration of the encounter.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A helmet made to protect the throat, neck and the rest of the head.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The throat is the most exposed point in a standard helmet configuration, the Guard Bascinet's designers understood that. The full enclosure was reserved for the Church's innermost security detail, the soldiers stationed where no one should need to be stopped. The fact that they exist implies something worth protecting. The fact that protection this thorough is needed implies something worth stopping.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

         <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_leather_jvest.png" alt="Leather Vest"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Leather Vest</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>23</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Entry-level body armour with substantial defense for its weight class. No movement penalties and no MS modifiers, sits at the ideal balance point for characters who need protection without the commitment of plate. Found in village settlements and Conrad's workshop stock. Upgradeable to Hard Leather Armor.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A vest made out of leather.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Militia standard-issue. The village militias received Leather Vests instead of iron plate because the Church prioritized the production of iron for its own soldiers. The vest was enough for most of what the militia encountered. For the things the militia encountered that it was not enough for, those incidents are in the Church's records under different classifications. The vests are in the field.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_loincloth.png" alt="Loincloth"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Loincloth</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>1</span></span><span class="stat-pill">DMG <span>1</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">The only armour piece in the game with an attached DMG value, the Loincloth's minimal profile leaves limbs free in ways that heavier armour restricts, enabling a +1 unarmed damage bonus as a side effect of wearing essentially nothing. DEF 1 provides token protection. Speed modifiers are maximally favorable. This is the speed-run loadout's body slot of choice.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A strip of weathered fabric designed for maximum mobility and minimal modesty.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The description was written by whoever wrote all the inventory text, and it is the only one with a detectable sense of humor. Whether the humor is intentional or whether the description is simply accurate, maximum mobility and minimal modesty are both true statements, depends on how the player reads it. The lore of the Loincloth is that it is, despite everything, the most honest piece of armor in the game. It does not pretend to be anything it is not.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_hard_leather_armor.png" alt="Hard Leather Armor"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Hard Leather Armor</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>24</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Upgraded form of the Leather Vest, one additional defense point with no additional weight cost. The hardening treatment stiffens the leather to resist edge damage more effectively than the untreated vest. Craftable at Conrad's workshop from Leather Scraps and Iron Ingot. A lateral upgrade rather than a dramatic one, but meaningful in sustained combat.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A slightly harder vest made out of leather.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The hardening process was Conrad's innovation, he developed it after a militia member brought him a vest that had failed. He studied the failure point and adjusted the treatment. He did not file the improvement with the Church's armory specification board. He simply made better armor for the people who came to him. That is the full extent of Conrad's resistance to institutional oversight. It is more than it sounds like.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_black_dress.png" alt="Black Dress"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Black Dress</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>1</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Memory item — functions identically to the Red Scarf in mechanical terms. Minimal defense, worn for reasons that have nothing to do with protection. Holding or equipping the Black Dress modifies certain NPC dialogue trees in ways that are not telegraphed, characters who knew the person who wore it respond differently when they see the player carrying it.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Reminds you of a distant past.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Black Dress and the Red Scarf share a description structure, both invoke a distant past, both provide nominal defense, both are clearly not armor in any practical sense. They are things someone kept because you do not throw away what remains of someone. The Black Dressed Doll in the accessory slot is a companion piece. The two items, equipped together, trigger a suppressed memory cutscene. The game does not tell you this is possible.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_iron_cuirass.png" alt="Iron Cuirass"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Iron Cuirass</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>27</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body Armour</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Mid-tier plate body armour. One defense point below the Plate Armour but available earlier in the game as a Holy Knight drop. The iron plate construction covers the torso comprehensively. Heavier than leather options, applies a minor speed penalty in sprint-based movement. Pairs well with the Guard Bascinet and Iron Shield for a full iron configuration.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A plated armour that offers a moderate protection.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Church's second-tier soldiers wore Iron Cuirasses, above the militia, below the elite Holy Knights. The tier distinction mattered to the Church administratively. In practice, the Cuirass-wearers were deployed where the work was consistent and dangerous but not prestigious. They are not in the records of notable engagements. They were in the engagements. They are just not notable in the records.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_body_armour_trench_coat.png" alt="Trench Coat"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Trench Coat</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Body</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>7</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Body</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Civilian garment with incidental armor value. The Trench Coat's primary game-mechanical function is environmental, in weather-affected zones, it prevents a slow MS accumulation caused by exposure. It is one of the only items that addresses weather as a mechanic. Low defense makes it a poor combat choice, but in the right environment, what it prevents is more significant than what it blocks.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A gray coat made to protect you from rain when in the trenches.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The trenches referenced in the description are not a location in the game. They are a historical reference, a conflict the Church fought in the Northern Reaches that is listed in suppressed documents but not in official Church history. The Trench Coat exists as evidence that the trenches existed. No character in the game makes this connection explicitly. Some players do.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>
	
	      <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_shield_wooden_buckler.png" alt="Wooden Buckler"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Wooden Buckler</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Shield</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>12</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Shield</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Entry-level shield. Occupies the off-hand slot, compatible with all one-handed weapons. The block mechanic reduces incoming damage by the shield's DEF value when timed correctly. Wooden construction means it can be broken by heavy blunt weapons after sustained blocking, the only shield with a destruction condition. Craftable from Wooden Planks.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A simple make-shift shield.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The simplest thing between a person and what is coming toward them. Whoever made the first Wooden Buckler did so quickly, with what was available, because waiting for the right materials was not an option. This describes a substantial portion of survival decisions in Elucidate. The Buckler is not built to last. It is built to last long enough.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="armour">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/armour_shield_scutum.png" alt="Scutum"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Scutum</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-armour">Shield</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">DEF <span>16</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Shield</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Mid-tier shield. Rectangular curved profile covers more body area than the round Iron Shield, superior against ranged projectiles that the round shield's edges might deflect only partially. The curved face allows projectiles to slide along the surface rather than impact flat. Found as Church soldier drops in mid-game encounters.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A rectangular, and curved shield made out of cast iron.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">An older design than the circular iron shields the Church's modern infantry carry, the Scutum's rectangular form comes from a pre-Church military tradition that valued formation coverage over individual mobility. Using a Scutum in the current era means carrying a design philosophy that the Church officially replaced. The shield does not know it is historical. It just works.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- BOOKS -->
      <div class="category-group" id="books">
        <div class="category-label">
          <span class="category-label-text">📖 Books & Skill Texts</span>
          <div class="category-label-line"></div>
        </div>
        <div class="item-cards-grid">

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_crafsmanship.png" alt="Book of Craftsmanship"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Craftsmanship</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Passive Skill</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Reading unlocks the Craftsmanship ability, enabling item crafting at any blacksmith station. Available in the Walled City armory chest and again in the Dormitory Ruins of the Home Village. Foundational, almost every weapon upgrade requires it.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Walled City copy was left in the armory by the Third Regiment, unclaimed. The copy in the Home Village ruins has marginal notes in a child's handwriting. The notes are practical observations about metalwork. Whoever wrote them was paying close attention. The Church had not yet taught them not to.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_stars.png" alt="Book of Stars"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Stars</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Active Skill</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks the Foresight skill. Reading it costs MS +5, the knowledge of what is coming is not free. Foresight reveals enemy positions, trap locations, and hidden NPC information. Its reliability degrades with MS: perfect clarity below 50, functional above 50, dangerously unreliable above 75.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Pluvia's people did not learn Foresight from this book. They held the knowledge before the book existed. The book is a Church-adjacent transcription of something the Tribe understood natively, translated into academic language that removes the felt sense of it entirely and replaces it with procedure. Pluvia, if asked about the book, says: <em>"Reading that is like being given a map of a country you have already walked."</em></div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_instincts.png" alt="Book of Instincts"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Instincts</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Active Skill</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks Battle Instinct — briefly marks enemy weak points for amplified damage. Father Calum uses this skill in Phase 2 of his boss fight. For player characters, it is particularly valuable against heavily armored targets where exploiting openings matters more than raw damage output.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Church produced this book internally. It was written by the program administrators to codify what the experiments produced, instincts that had been trained into subjects so deeply they could no longer be separated from identity. Father Calum co-authored it. He is listed as a technical consultant. His co-author was Subject C-2, who did not survive the procedure that made Battle Instinct possible to document.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_trade_i.png" alt="Book of Trade I"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Trade I — III</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Passive Skill</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">A series of three books unlocking Bartering, Bargaining, and Rumours respectively. Konrad begins with Bartering; the full chain reduces purchase prices and opens alternative information-gathering in tavern interactions. Book of Trade III (Rumours) unlocks NPC whisper dialogue unavailable through standard conversation.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Guild Hall stocks all three. The Guild Master who warns Konrad about pressing the Church investigation has read all three volumes many times. He knows what they are for. He also knows that information has a price paid not in coins but in the kind of quiet that follows knowing too much. He made his choice twenty years ago and lives with it. Konrad made his more recently.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_the_secrets.png" alt="Book of the Secrets"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of the Secrets</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Lore Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Reading grants no skill, only knowledge. The game's most explicit lore item regarding Church hypocrisy: it states plainly that the Church worships a false God while actually embracing darkness. No mechanics attached. Some information changes nothing and everything simultaneously.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The book exists in the Restricted Corridor of the Theocratic Capital, behind a door that Father Calum opens with a key he carries on his person at all times. He has not read this book in eleven years, by choice. It was written by one of the original Church founders, someone who built the institution knowing exactly what they were building and chose the name Lumen as irony, not faith. The founder's name has been removed from every historical record. Someone named F has written in the margin: <em>"Both cages. Still cages."</em></div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_healing.png" alt="Book of Healing"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Healing</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Active Skill</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks the Healing active skill, allowing recovery of HP outside combat. The description calls it a book that teaches the doctrine of Lumen, meaning the Church's canonical theology is the instruction manual for keeping yourself alive. The game does not comment on this irony directly. It simply states it.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Lumen doctrine teaches that light cures corruption. The experiments the Church ran on children suggest a different interpretation: that what they called <em>light</em> was the process of removing whatever the darkness had touched, regardless of whether removing it left anything behind. The Book of Healing teaches restoration. What the Church practiced was subtraction. These are different things that the book does not reconcile, because the book was written before the experiments began.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>
		  
          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_marksmanship.png" alt="Book of Marksmanship"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Marksmanship</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Skill Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Passive Skill Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Teaches Marksmanship</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Reading this book permanently unlocks the Marksmanship passive skill. Marksmanship increases effective accuracy and critical-hit chance for all ranged weapons, bows, crossbows, and firearms. The passive activates without equipping and stacks with the Rapid Fire active skill. Essential for ranged-focused builds and required for optimal Musket performance.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A book about the art of marksmanship.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The Church maintained a dedicated marksmanship training program for two decades before the regiment was reorganized. The curriculum was formalized in this text, a practical guide that treats aiming as philosophy: patience, breath, the relationship between stillness and movement. Whoever wrote it understood that hitting a target reliably requires understanding why you are missing first. The Church used the text. They did not learn the lesson in the last chapter.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_rapid_fire.png" alt="Book of Rapid Fire"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Rapid Fire</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Skill Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Active Skill Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Teaches Rapid Fire</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks the Rapid Fire active skill upon reading. Rapid Fire dramatically reduces draw and reload time for all bow-class weapons for a limited duration, transforming the Short Bow from a controlled precision tool into a sustained suppression weapon. Consumes stamina while active. Does not apply to crossbow or firearm weapons, which have mechanical rather than muscular draw cycles.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A book detailing how to draw a bow faster.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">Speed in archery is counterintuitive, drawing faster requires more controlled relaxation of the draw arm, not more force. This book exists because that counterintuition needs to be written down; it does not come naturally to soldiers trained to associate effort with result. Someone figured out the contradiction and documented it. The chapter on breathing is longer than the chapter on technique. This is correct.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_agility.png" alt="Book of Agility"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Agility</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Skill Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Active Skill Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Teaches Agility Augmentation</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks Agility Augmentation — an active skill that temporarily boosts dodge speed, attack animation recovery, and general movement speed simultaneously. The combined effect creates a brief window of near-untouchable mobility ideal for repositioning in multi-enemy encounters. Pairs with Swift Boots for a combined speed effect that significantly changes how combat flows.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A book about the art of being fast in battles.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The author's position is stated in the preface: speed is not a natural gift distributed unequally, but a skill set developed through structured understanding of how the body moves when it is afraid versus when it is not. The book is about reducing the fear-response lag, the fraction of a second between perceiving danger and acting on it. In Elucidate, that fraction of a second is represented mechanically. The book closes it.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_cowardice_i.png" alt="Book of Cowardice I"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Cowardice I</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Skill Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Active Skill Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Teaches Escape</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks the Escape active skill — a one-use-per-encounter ability that immediately removes the character from melee range with a guaranteed distance break regardless of enemy AI state. Essential for encounters where the combat math does not favor engagement. Particularly useful in the Underground Laboratory's ambush configurations where initial positioning is already compromised.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A book about escaping if the battle is against your odds.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The title is intentional. Whoever named this text called it cowardice knowing that the word would sting, and wrote it anyway, because what they were teaching was more important than whether readers were comfortable with the framing. Knowing when you cannot win and acting on that knowledge requires a different kind of courage than staying. The Church does not teach this. The Church teaches soldiers to stay. Some of those soldiers did not need to.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="item-entry" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_cowardice_ii.png" alt="Book of Cowardice II"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Cowardice II</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Skill Book</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Active Skill Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Teaches Sprint</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Unlocks Sprint — an active skill providing a sustained movement speed burst with extended duration compared to the base dodge-roll. Where Escape is for instant disengagement, Sprint is for creating sustained distance. Ideal for the open zones where pursuit AI has a longer tracking range. Stacks with Swift Boots for combined mobility that most enemy AI cannot match at full speed.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">A book about how running away from a battle is tactical.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The second volume makes the philosophical argument explicit that the first only implied: survival is legitimate. Running away is not failure; it is the preservation of the capacity to make different choices later. The author does not argue against fighting. They argue against fighting when fighting is the only option that has been made available. The distinction is the entire book. The description is its thesis, stated plainly. <em>Running away from a battle is ok.</em> Someone needed to write that down.</div></div>
            </div>
          </div>

         <div class="item-entry caligo-item" data-category="book">
            <div class="item-img-wrap"><img src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/materials_skill_book_of_pestilence_i.png" alt="Book of Pestilence I"><span class="item-symbol"></span><div class="item-img-overlay"></div></div>
            <div class="item-body">
              <div class="item-header">
                <div class="item-name-block"><div class="item-name">Book of Pestilence I</div></div>
                <span class="item-type-badge badge-book">Pestilence</span>
              </div>
              <div class="item-stats"><span class="stat-pill">Type <span>Status Book</span></span><span class="stat-pill">Effect <span>Bleeding</span></span></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Function</div><div class="item-section-text">Reading this book inflicts the Bleeding status on the reader. Bleeding causes continuous HP loss per turn until treated with appropriate materials. The status cannot be resisted through equipment, it bypasses all DEF values and interacts directly with the HP pool. The only counter is Green Herb-based remedies. Do not read unless the consequence is understood.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Description</div><div class="item-section-text">Causes bleeding when read.</div></div>
              <div class="item-section"><div class="item-section-label">Lore</div><div class="item-section-text lore">The eight Books of Pestilence are the most unambiguous items in the game. The description is a warning. The warning is accurate. There is no hidden benefit, no conditional upside, no lore reward for reading them.
