      <!-- ══ THEOCRATIC CAPITAL ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry dual-zone" id="theocratic-capital">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 02</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Theocratic Capital</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">Safe Zone (First Visit) — Final Battlefield (Ending B) — Starting Point (Priest)</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-dual">Dual-State Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: +0 / +8</span>
          </div>
        </div>

		<div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-THEOCRATIC-CAPITAL.jpg" 
			alt="Walled City Environment" 
			class="region-img">
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Theocratic Capital — Dual-State Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Grand Gothic Architecture Hiding Rot</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Pristine white marble. Gold-tinted stained glass.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Rich candlelight. Dark restricted corridors.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Choir hymns, tolling bells, whispering priests.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Second Visit</div><div class="atmo-val">Battle erupts: knights, assassins, revolution.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Theocratic Capital is the most beautiful location in the game and the most dishonest. Its architects were given one instruction: build something that makes people feel small in comparison to the Church's authority. They succeeded completely. Every column is proportioned to make humans look like children. Every hall echoes in a way that makes private thought feel like trespass. The candlelight in the nave creates shadows that seem to move with purpose, tracking visitors who do not belong.</p>
              <p>On a second visit, during Ending B, the beauty ruptures. The marble runs with blood. The choir's voices are replaced by screaming. Holy Knights pour from the administrative wing with the organized violence of men who have been waiting a long time to do exactly this.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Church's Crowning Hypocrisy</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The capital was built on the site of a pre-Lumen settlement, a community of scholars who had, ironically, been attempting to understand Caligo academically before the Church declared such inquiry heretical and buried the ruins beneath new foundations. The library in the Main Cathedral still contains books from that original settlement, misfiled by accident and never discovered because no Church librarian would think to look for pre-Lumen scholarship in a Lumen holy site.</p>
              <p>Father Calum rules this place not with warmth but with precision. He knows every room, every corridor, every confession booth where someone said something they should not have. He has been administering the Underground Laboratories beneath the capital for decades, and the calm in his voice when he discusses the experiments suggests a man who has redefined his own conscience so thoroughly that it no longer troubles him. He was, after all, Subject C-1, the first fully successful outcome of the very experiments he now oversees.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">🧠</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">First visit: +0 (holy atmosphere initially suppresses psychological deterioration). Second visit (Ending B): +8 from battle trauma and the revelation of the Church's true nature. Priest DLC: each experiment session in the Labyrinth beneath raises Mind State significantly.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">

                <div class="segment-name">Cathedral Plaza</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Public area with pilgrims, merchants, and Church attendants. The outdoor market sells holy items to the faithful.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Public Fountain in the plaza is fed by an underground spring whose source runs directly beneath the Laboratory. The water has been tested by three independent merchants and found chemically pure. This has never stopped tasting wrong to those who know what flows nearby.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">

					 style="background: url('http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Main-Cathedral-scaled.png') center/cover no-repeat;
							display:flex;
							align-items:center;

							font-size:8px;

				</div>

                <div class="segment-desc">The grand church. Mission assignment occurs at the Priest's Altar. The library contains lore books if the player seeks them. Confession booths hold cryptic NPC dialogue about the experiments.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Library's restricted section contains records from the founding years, records that the current Church leadership believes were destroyed. The Librarian Scholar has read them all and teaches from them obliquely, in metaphors, to anyone patient enough to listen to his entire lecture.</div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
				<div class="placeholder-region" 
					 style="background: url('http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Theocratic-Battleground-Ending-B-scaled.png') center/cover no-repeat;
							display:flex;

							justify-content:center;

				</div>

              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Theocratic Battleground</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Ending B exclusive. The capital erupts into battle as revolution overtakes Church authority. The Cathedral Nave becomes the arena for the final confrontation with Father Calum.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">During the battle, loyal citizens take up arms against the Mercenary, people who were never bad, only entirely convinced. Their faces show grief more than hatred. They die defending something that was never what they were told it was.</div>

        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- ══ HOME VILLAGE ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry danger-zone" id="home-village">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 03</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Home Village</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">High-Risk Zone — Trauma Zone — Mind State Accelerator</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-danger">High-Risk Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: ×2 Rate</span>
          </div>
        </div>
		<div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-HOME-VILLAGE.jpg" 
			alt="Home Village" 
			class="region-img">
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Home Village — High-Risk Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Ruins of What You Were</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Ash grey-brown. Permanent overcast sky.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">No direct sunlight. Dying torches flicker.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Wind through ruins. Distant eldritch groaning.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">Eldritch Horrors. Structural collapse zones.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Everything here burned. Not quickly, the ash still carries the particular smell of wood that was given time to become something else before it stopped burning entirely. The village center is three years of overgrowth consuming what the fire left. The orphanage at the edge stands longest, because it was built to last, because someone wanted what happened inside it to last, and now it stands as evidence that permanence and goodness have no necessary relationship.</p>
              <p>Eldritch Horrors patrol the ruins in humanoid form, their shapes suggesting what they once were without confirming it. At low Mind State they are disturbing. At higher Mind State they become recognizable, and that is worse.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Where Everything Began</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The village was not destroyed by the Cult. It was not destroyed by a war. It was destroyed by a retrieval operation, Church soldiers who came not to burn but to collect, and burned only because what they found was messier than anticipated. The children the orphanage sheltered were the Church's raw material: orphans without family connections, without anyone who would ask questions, without anyone whose silence could not be assured through careful administrative procedure.</p>
              <p>The Mercenary grew up here. So did the Cultist. So did Pluvia the Shaman, and Konrad the Merchant, and Father Calum himself, though he returned differently than he left. The village is where all five protagonists were first made into subjects. Every playthrough, regardless of character, traces back to these ruins. Every answer the game offers has its roots in this ash.</p>
              <p>The orphanage headmaster's office contains a note written in a hand that the Mercenary will recognize subconsciously without being able to explain why. The note says: "The successful ones will not remember. The unsuccessful ones will remember everything."</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">⚠️</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect — DOUBLED ACCELERATION</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">+15 on entry from traumatic environment. Each Eldritch Horror encounter: +8. Each memory fragment triggered: +5. Extended stay: +3 per in-game minute. The Home Village is the first true test of Mind State management. No Blue Gems available here.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
				<img 
				src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Home-Village-Entry-scaled.png" 
				alt="Village Entry" 
				class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Village Entry</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Village Entry</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Entry to the ruined village. Signs of battle and fire everywhere. A collapsed gate frames the first view of what remains. The mass grave area should not be looked at directly but cannot be avoided.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The burnt farmhouses still contain salvageable items. Someone lived here, had tools, had food, had ordinary plans for the following week. The ordinary weight of that is its own form of horror.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Leather Scraps</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Raw Iron</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Green Herb</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
				<img 
				src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/The-Old-Orphanage-scaled.png" 
				alt="Old Orphanage" 
				class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">The Old Orphanage</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">The Old Orphanage</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">The Mercenary's childhood home. The front triggers a tutorial memory. The dormitory holds skill books. A hidden basement documents what was done to the children who lived here. The headmaster's office contains the most important single piece of paper in the game's first act.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The hidden basement connects conceptually to the Underground Laboratory, though physically they are not linked. The same hands designed both. The same logic justified both. One was practice; the other was purpose.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Craftsmanship Skill Book</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Experimental Notes</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Empty Vials</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="items-found reveal">
          <div class="items-found-title">All Items Found in Home Village</div>

      <!-- ══ OUTSKIRTS VILLAGE ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry" id="outskirts-village">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 04</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Outskirts Village</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">Safe Zone — Transition Hub</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-safe">Safe Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: −3 (Recovery)</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-OUTSKIRTS-VILLAGE.jpg" 
			alt="Outskirt Village" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Outskirts Village — Safe Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Warmth Before the Storm</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Warm earth tones. Daytime sunlight.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Warm daytime sun. Interior candlelight.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Birdsong, children, marketplace chatter.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">None. NPCs anxious about war.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>After Home Village's ash and silence, the Outskirts Village is almost unbearably ordinary. Children run in the streets. A merchant argues about the price of grain. Someone is mending a fence that does not urgently need mending but gives them something to do with their hands while they worry. The contrast is not accidental, this is what the war is being fought over, or what both sides claim they are fighting for, while neither side particularly considers what the people in the middle of it actually need.</p>
              <p>The village knows about the Tribe but will not say so directly. NPCs speak about the forest to the north with carefully calibrated non-specificity: it is a fine forest, there are many trees in it, one should be careful of the paths. The Village Chief speaks more plainly, but only after trust is established.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Silence of Ordinary People</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Outskirts Village has survived the war by practicing a disciplined collective discretion. When Church soldiers pass through, the village presents itself as loyal and unremarkable. When the Cult sends scouts, the village presents itself as too poor to be worth intimidating. This is not cowardice, it is a strategy for survival refined over generations by people who learned early that visibility is dangerous when powerful forces are in conflict.</p>
              <p>The traveling bard who appears in the village square sings songs about Caligo that could be interpreted as historical folk music by anyone who did not know better. He is not Church, not Cult. He is something older, a carrier of the stories that both sides want forgotten, wrapped in melodies pleasant enough that no one thinks to silence him.</p>
              <p>The Village Chief's hidden basement passage connects to the Tribe through a route that has been maintained in secret for years. It is the most important door in the Outskirts Village and looks like a floor. This is intentional.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning" style="border-color:rgba(74,170,102,0.3); background:rgba(74,170,102,0.05);">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">🌿</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title" style="color:#4aaa66">Mind State Effect — Recovery Zone</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">No Mind State increase. Outskirts Village acts as a psychological recovery zone, the resting mechanic reduces Mind State by −3. The only location in the game where passive MS reduction occurs naturally.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Village-Market-Village-Outskirts-scaled.png" 
			alt="Village Market" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Village Market</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Village Market</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Small but stocked market. Food stall, general merchant, and a notice board offering optional side quests. The opportunity to rest and resupply after Home Village.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The merchants here buy and sell without asking where things came from. This policy exists because asking where things come from, in wartime, frequently leads to answers that make commerce impossible.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Ale / Rum / Wine</span>

                    <span class="item-tag">Bowstring</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Ext.-Chief_s-Home-scaled.png" 
			alt="Village Chief's Home" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Village Chief's Home</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Village Chief's Home</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Key location. The Chief's dialogue unlocks access to the hidden basement trapdoor that connects underground to the Tribe Area. The most important floor in the village.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Chief has been protecting this passage for eleven years. He built the room above the trapdoor himself, specifically to make it look like every other room in the house. He has had the conversation that happens next many times and has not yet gotten better at it.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Dirk (via trade)</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Leather Scraps</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

      <!-- ══ TRIBE AREA ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry" id="tribe-area">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 05</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Tribe Area</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">Safe Zone — Timed Rescue Event — Starting Point (Shaman)</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-safe">Safe Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: +5/encounter</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-TRIBE.jpg" 
			alt="Seekers Tribe" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Tribe Area — Timed Safe Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Ancient and Hidden, Now Under Attack</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Earthy tones. Natural firelight. Rocky terrain.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Natural firelight and torches. No Church influence.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Tribal chants, crackling fires, urgent battle sounds.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">Church attack forces. 30-minute countdown.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Tribe's settlement is carved into rock formations that the Church's maps don't acknowledge. It exists in the negative space of the official cartography, a place that should be wilderness according to Church records and is instead a living community. Tribal symbols mark the walls in pigments that predate the Church by centuries. The firelight is warm. The Tribe Elder's face suggests an old argument with the world that the world has not yet won.</p>
              <p>During the rescue sequence, the warmth cracks. Church soldiers pour through the perimeter watchtowers. Every thirty minutes, if the Shaman is not secured and extracted, she dies. The game does not soften this. The timer does not pause for dialogue. The Tribe does not wait for the player to be ready.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Survivors of the Purge</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Tribe exists because of a single act of mercy performed by a Church soldier two decades before the game's events, a soldier who, given orders to eliminate the settlement, chose instead to file a report stating that no settlement had been found. He is long dead. The Tribe does not know his name. They know only that someone, once, declined to do the thing they were ordered to do.</p>
              <p>Pluvia, the Shaman, was brought here after she escaped the Underground Laboratory as a child. Her Foresight ability, a result of the Church's experiments, has been both her protection and her burden. She has seen the next three weeks of any future she focuses on since she was nine years old. She has never been able to unsee anything she has seen. This is why the Tribe Elder looks at her sometimes with a specific kind of grief that has no word.</p>
              <p>The Fragment of Caligo stored in the cave was not placed here by the Tribe. It arrived. It moved, over years, from the eastern corruption zones, through the forest, and settled in the cave as if it had chosen to. The Tribe Elder does not discuss this. She treats it as she treats other things that arrive without being invited: with caution, with respect, and without looking directly at it.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">⏱️</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect — Rescue Sequence</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">During rescue: +5 per Church soldier encounter. Failure to save Shaman: +20 Mind State spike — the single largest non-Zone event in the game. If saved, Shaman's presence reduces Mercenary's MS growth rate by 30% for the remainder of the playthrough.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Tribe-Perimeter-scaled.png" 
			alt="Seekers Tribe" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Tribe Perimeter</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Tribe Perimeter</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Outer defenses carved into rocky terrain. Watchtowers are the first contact points with Church forces during the rescue sequence. Ambush Alley requires combat or evasion.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The watchtowers were built facing outward, toward the forest, watching for Church scouts. During the rescue, Church soldiers come from the same direction the Tribe has been watching for years. They were expected. Not expected to succeed.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Red Vial</span></div></div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Tribe-Settlement-scaled.png" 
			alt="Seekers Tribe" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Tribe Settlement</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Tribe Settlement</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Core living area. The Healing Hut holds Pluvia. The Storage Cave contains the Fragment of Caligo. The Tribe Chief offers Wind Essence after the successful rescue.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Communal Fire Pit has been burning continuously for sixty years. The Tribe considers its flame a living entity distinct from the wood that feeds it. Eldritch scholars would find this interpretation more accurate than poetic.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Fragment of Caligo</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Wind Essence</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Blue Gem</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Green Herb</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="items-found reveal">
          <div class="items-found-title">All Items Found in Tribe Area</div>
          <div class="items-grid">
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">🌑</span>Fragment of Caligo</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">💨</span>Wind Essence</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">💎</span>Blue Gem</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">🌿</span>Green Herb (×3)</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">🧪</span>Red Vial</span>
          </div>

      </div>

      <!-- ══ PORT CITY ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry" id="port-city">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 06</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Port City</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">Safe Zone — Key Decision Hub — Starting Point (Merchant)</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-safe">Safe Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: +3 (Revelation)</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Port-CIty-Portrait-1-scaled.png" 
			alt="Port City" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Port City — Key Decision Hub</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The City That Trades in Everything</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Bright coastal light. Lantern-lit taverns at night.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Coastal sun by day. Warm market glow by night.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Seagulls, ship bells, tavern music, crowd noise.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">None. Church spies observe. Konrad waits.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Port City is the loudest location in the game, and the most comfortable, and the most dangerous in ways that have nothing to do with enemies. The Merchant Guild controls the peace here through economic leverage rather than force, no one fights in a city where fighting disrupts trade, and the Guild has ensured that every resident's livelihood depends on trade continuing undisrupted. It is an impressive achievement and a deeply fragile one.</p>
              <p>The Gilded Anchor tavern at the heart of the Merchant Quarter is where the world's decisions get made by proxy. Information flows here. Konrad Merchant has been sitting in the same corner booth for twenty years, gathering the evidence for a case that the Church will never allow to be tried, waiting for someone who can actually do something with what he knows.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Weight of What Konrad Knows</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Konrad was at the orphanage. He was there when the Church soldiers came. He was seven years old and small enough to fit under the floor of the cellar and intelligent enough to stay silent for six hours while everything above him was resolved. What he witnessed he has spent twenty years translating into evidence, shipping records, financial documents, testimony from survivors he tracked down one by one across the continent.</p>
              <p>The decision that occurs in Port City, whether to believe Konrad's revelation or dismiss it, is the central path lock of the game. It is framed as an information question but it is actually a question about the player's character: can you hold a truth that requires everything you have been told to be wrong? The game does not judge either answer. It simply shows you where each road goes.</p>
              <p>Church spies watch the Guild Hall from a hidden office behind a bookshelf in the harbor district. They have been watching long enough that they know about Konrad. They have never moved against him because his death would make his evidence more credible, not less. The Church is patient. It has had a lot of practice.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning" style="border-color:rgba(184,146,42,0.3); background:rgba(184,146,42,0.05);">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">💡</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title" style="color:var(--gold)">Mind State Effect — Revelation Shock</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">No Mind State increase in Port City itself. However, if Konrad's revelations are accepted, the emotional weight of the truth causes a minor +3 Mind State from revelation shock. This is the only Mind State increase in the game triggered purely by knowledge rather than threat.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Harbor-District-scaled.png"

			class="region-img">

                <div class="segment-img-label">Harbor District</div>

              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Harbor District</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Docking bay, customs office, and warehouse row. Arriving travelers and shipping cargo flow through here. The Harbor Captain offers passage to Cultist Island for certain paths.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The warehouse district contains shipments the customs office has been paid not to examine. This is openly acknowledged and never spoken aloud, a condition of commerce that everyone in the harbor maintains through tacit agreement.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Iron Ingots</span><span class="item-tag">Silver Wire</span></div></div>

              </div>

                <div class="segment-name">Church Spy Outpost</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Hidden behind a bookshelf in the harbor district. The Church observers have documented every Guild transaction for years. Spy documents reveal the Church's true motives and interest in the Merchant Guild.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The escape ladder at the back of the outpost has never been used. Whoever built it assumed this observation post would one day need evacuating. The fact that it has not needed evacuating does not mean nothing bad has happened here, it means the bad things have been authorized.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Spy Documents</span><span class="item-tag">Red Gem</span></div></div>

              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Merchant Quarter</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Heart of the city. Merchant Guild Hall, the Gilded Anchor tavern, general market, and bank. Konrad's permanent base. The central path lock interaction happens in the Guild Hall.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The tavern keeper at The Gilded Anchor has been brokering information for thirty years. He knows where every significant person in Port City has been, what they bought, who they spoke to. He is the most dangerous man in the city and presents himself as a man who refills cups.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>

      <!-- ══ CULTIST ISLAND ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry caligo-zone" id="cultist-island">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 07</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Cultist Island</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">High-Risk Zone — Final Mission Area — Starting Point (Cultist)</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-danger">High-Risk Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-caligo">Caligo Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: Fastest</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cultist-Island-Portrait-scaled.png" 
			alt="Cultist Island" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Cultist Island — Caligo Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The World as Caligo Prefers It</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Purple-black. Organic architecture. Wrongness.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Pulsing bioluminescent growths. Flickering.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Cult chants, distant screaming, heartbeat ambience.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">Passive corruption damage at very high MS. Eldritch Manifestations at 75%+.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Cultist Island does not look like a place people chose to live. It looks like a place that chose them, a geography that grew to accommodate its inhabitants by becoming something they could feel at home in, which means something no one else could stand to look at for long. The architecture is partially stone and partially something else: growth patterns that follow no biological logic, surfaces that pulse slowly under touch, structures that cast shadows in directions the light does not suggest.</p>
              <p>Caligo's influence is most concentrated here. The bioluminescent growths that serve as light sources respond to Mind State levels, brightening and pulsing faster as the player's psychological stability decreases. At very high Mind State, the light begins to look like breathing. This is not a graphical effect, the growths are alive, in whatever sense Caligo's expressions can be described as alive.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Church's Other Children</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Cult of Caligo was not founded. It accumulated. Failed experiments who survived their abandonment, soldiers left in corrupted zones who adapted instead of dying, civilians from villages the Church destroyed who found that Caligo's presence offered something the Church never had: an acknowledgment that the darkness was real, that it could not be prayed away, and that survival within it was possible if you stopped pretending it was not there.</p>
              <p>Funeris, the Cultist, was expelled from the Church at nine years old when her experimental results proved too unstable for their purposes. The Cult took her in not out of compassion but out of pragmatism: she was useful to them. Over time the relationship became more complex than utility. In Path A she is an obstacle. In the Cultist DLC she is the lens through which everything else in the game is reread, because from her perspective, the Mercenary is not a hero, and the Church is not a villain to be defeated. Both of them are just more children from the same orphanage, trying to make sense of what was done to them.</p>
              <p>The largest Fragment of Caligo rests in the Cult Leader's fortress vault. The Cult did not bring it there. It arrived the same way the Tribe Area's fragment arrived, the same way the Port City fragment surfaced, the same way all of them have been moving, slowly, over years, toward the people most entangled with Caligo's influence. They are not tools. They are not weapons. They are, as the Library of Caligo's most carefully preserved text states, "the places where Caligo remembers having been."</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">💀</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect — FASTEST ZONE</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">+5 per minute of exploration. Each Cultist combat: +6. Eldritch Manifestation encounter: +12. Blue Gems scattered throughout are critical resources. Path C: Mind State reaches 100 during this zone — insanity ending triggered. The island is designed to be unmanageable without careful resource preparation.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">

                <div class="segment-desc">Entry point. Cult watchers launch an immediate ambush from the shore. The Signal Tower can be disabled for a stealth approach. A coastal cave offers an alternate entry path for careful players.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The shore has no natural harbor. Ships dock by driving ashore and the beach reforms around them within hours, as if the island accepts arrivals it has chosen and refuses those it has not.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Scimitar (drop)</span><span class="item-tag">Blue Gem</span></div></div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">

			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Inner-Sanctum-Cult-scaled.png" 
			alt="Cultist Island" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Inner Sanctum</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Inner Sanctum</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Core cult territory. Heavily guarded. The Library of Caligo here contains the most complete lore documentation about Caligo's nature in the entire game. Prison cells hold experimented subjects with critical backstory.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Library was assembled over decades from every source the Cult could obtain, Church records stolen during raids, scholarly texts from pre-Lumen civilizations, and direct transcriptions of what cult members experienced during deep Caligo communion. It is more complete than anything in the Theocratic Capital's library, and far more honest.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Ancient Paper</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Ink Vial</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Dark Priest's Robe</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Cult-Leader-Fortress-scaled.png" 
			alt="Cultist Island" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Cult Leader's Fortress</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Cult Leader's Fortress</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Final area. The Throne Room Approach is a single atmospheric corridor. The Boss Chamber houses the Cult Leader encounter. The Fragment Vault unlocks only after the leader is defeated.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The Cult Leader does not consider himself a villain. He considers himself the only person who looked at what the Church did to the orphans and reached the correct conclusion: that if the Church could use Caligo as a tool, Caligo could use the Church as a medium, and could, in time, use everything else.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">

              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

      <!-- ══ ZONES OF TERRORS ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry danger-zone" id="zones-of-terrors">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 08</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Zones of Terrors</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">High-Risk Zone — Eldritch Horror Area — Mind State Accelerator</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-danger">High-Risk Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: +3/min</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-SEEKERS-TRIBE.jpg" 
			alt="Zones of Terrors" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Cultist Island — Caligo Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Reality As It Remembers Being Wrong</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Palette</div><div class="atmo-val">Wrong colors. Skies that shouldn't be.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Lighting</div><div class="atmo-val">Pulsing eldritch glow. Purple and red hues.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Distorted ambience. Backward music. Wet organic sounds.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">Environmental corruption damage. Hallucination enemies at high MS.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Zones of Terrors are not a single location. They are the seams. Everywhere the world was sutured together after Caligo's disruption, the stitching shows. Trees lean against gravity. Reflections in water show places that are not there. Structures from eras that the world does not acknowledge stand partially dissolved into biological-looking formations, as if the world is slowly digesting the evidence that it once was otherwise.</p>
              <p>Eldritch Horrors are most concentrated here, and their appearance reflects the Mind State of the player observing them. At low MS they are recognizably wrong, humanoid in general shape, incorrect in proportion and motion. As MS increases they become more detailed in their wrongness: additional limbs, composite faces, movements that suggest two bodies operating a single form. At maximum Mind State, they look recognizably human, and that is the point at which they become hardest to fight.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Memory Scars of the World</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Subject I-3's passage through the world created the first Zones of Terrors, but it did not create all of them. The zones spread because the conditions for their creation, places where the membrane between the world's order and its underlying chaos grew thin, were pre-existing. Subject I-3 did not corrupt the world. The world was already seamed. I-3 simply showed where the seams were under sufficient pressure.</p>
              <p>The Caligo Shrines found in the deep zones were not built by the Cult. Scholars who have reached them describe them as pre-construction, formations that assembled themselves from the surrounding material over decades, accreting meaning the way mineral deposits accrete minerals. They store no blessing, give no power. Touching them simply costs something, which is the most honest transaction in the game.</p>
              <p>The Zones of Terrors are heaviest in the Cultist and Shaman DLCs because both characters have a fundamental relationship with Caligo that the Mercenary does not. What threatens the Mercenary from outside is navigated from within by Funeris and Pluvia, they are not threatened by the Zones so much as recognized by them.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">👁️</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">+3 per in-game minute of traversal. +10 per Eldritch Horror combat. Nexus Points: +15 upon entering. Apex form encounters: +12. Hallucination enemies appear at MS 75+ and cannot be defeated, only survived. Designed to punish the unprepared while rewarding careful navigation.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Corrupted-Frontier-scaled.png" 
			alt="Zones of Terrors" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Corrupted Frontier</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Corrupted Frontier</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">The border zone where visual distortion begins. First Horror encounters. An abandoned waystation serves as a temporary rest point, the last normal architecture before the Zones proper begin.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The waystation's previous occupants left food and medical supplies arranged as if they had stepped out briefly. The food has not decayed at a normal rate. Time in the frontier zones does not behave consistently, this is not metaphor.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Red Vials</span><span class="item-tag">Blue Gem</span></div></div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Rare-Nexus-Points-Zone-of-Terror-scaled.png" 
			alt="Zones of Terrors" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Caligo Nexus Points</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Caligo Nexus Points</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">The game's most dangerous areas. Heaviest Caligo concentration. The Nexus Core triggers environmental events when Mind State is high. The Fragment Echo holds an optional Fragment shard for completionists.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">At high Mind State, the Nexus Core shows hallucinations drawn from the character's own history. These are not random, they are specific. The game does not explain how Caligo knows these things. This is intentional.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Fragment of Caligo (shard)</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Blue Gems</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Rare Eldritch Materials</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="items-found reveal">
          <div class="items-found-title">All Items Found in Zones of Terrors</div>
          <div class="items-grid">
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">💎</span>Blue Gems (critical)</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">🌑</span>Fragment of Caligo Shard</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">💀</span>Eldritch Horror Drops</span>
            <span class="item-chip"><span class="chip-icon">🧪</span>Red Vials</span>

      <!-- ══ UNDERGROUND LABS ══ -->
      <div class="region-entry caligo-zone" id="underground-labs">
        <div class="region-header">
          <div class="region-title-block">
            <div class="region-number">Region 09</div>
            <h2 class="region-name">Underground Orphan Laboratories</h2>
            <div class="region-subtitle">Forbidden Subterranean Labyrinth — Psychological Horror Area — Critical Lore Location</div>
          </div>
          <div class="region-badges">
            <span class="region-badge badge-danger">High-Risk Zone</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-caligo">Psychological Horror</span>
            <span class="region-badge badge-ms">MS: No Reducers</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-img-wrap reveal">
          <div class="placeholder-region" style="background: linear-gradient(135deg,#111510 0%,#1a201a 40%,#131510 100%);">
		  <img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Copy-of-FORBIDDEN-SUBTERRANEAN-LABYRINTH.jpg" 
			alt="Laboratory" 
			class="region-img">
          </div>
          <div class="region-img-overlay"></div>
          <div class="region-img-label">Underground Laboratories — Forbidden Zone</div>
        </div>

        <div class="region-body">
          <div class="region-description">
            <div class="rblock-label">Environment</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">Sterile Horror Meets Decay</div>
            <div class="atmo-grid">
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Old Section</div><div class="atmo-val">Rusted equipment. Water-stained stone. Bones.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">New Section</div><div class="atmo-val">Clinical white tiles stained with blood. Active machinery.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Sounds</div><div class="atmo-val">Muffled screaming. Machinery hum. Dripping water.</div></div>
              <div class="atmo-item"><div class="atmo-key">Hazards</div><div class="atmo-val">Illusion enemies. Experimental subjects as enemies. Priest's forces.</div></div>
            </div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>Two versions of the same nightmare exist simultaneously in the Underground Laboratories. The Old Section is what the Church built decades ago: stone corridors made wrong by their purpose, surgical theaters where the equipment has long since rusted but the stains remain to indicate what the equipment was for. Memory Fragments play as non-interactive sequences here, the past does not require the player's participation to continue being true.</p>
              <p>The New Section is active. It is clinical in the way that only institutions that have normalized their practices become clinical, everything labeled, everything organized, everything proceeding according to documented protocols that Father Calum reviews quarterly to ensure efficiency. Imprisoned subjects in the holding cells provide more information about the experiments than any document, if the player can bear to listen to them.</p>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div class="region-lore-block">
            <div class="rblock-label">Lore</div>
            <div class="rblock-title">The Truth the Church Built Its Walls Around</div>
            <div class="rblock-text">
              <p>The Underground Laboratory is the answer to every question the game has been asking. Where did the Mercenary's enhanced physiology come from? Why does the Shaman see the future in three-week intervals? How does Funeris the Cultist channel Caligo directly? Why is Father Calum immune to Mind State deterioration? Why does Konrad know what he knows?</p>
              <p>All five protagonists were experimented on here as children. The experiments used Fragments of Caligo as catalysts, exposing orphan subjects to controlled eldritch influence and observing what adaptations developed. Most subjects did not survive. Some survived in forms the Church could not use and were disposed of. The successful subjects, designated by series identifier, were either recruited into Church service (Calum, as Subject C-1) or escaped (the others).</p>
              <p>Father Calum's Office contains the documents that prove all of this in bureaucratic detail. The documents are written in the same calm, administrative tone as every other Church record, because they were written by the same people, following the same procedural standards, treating the experiments as a logistics problem rather than a moral one. The horror of the documents is not their content but their register: the Church did not lose its humanity doing this. It simply never consulted it.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="ms-warning">
          <div class="ms-warning-icon">🔬</div>
          <div class="ms-warning-content">
            <div class="ms-warning-title">Mind State Effect — NO REDUCTION AVAILABLE</div>
            <div class="ms-warning-text">+10 on entry. Each active chamber: +8. Witnessing Priest's operations: +12. Each experiment subject interaction: +5 from emotional weight. Old section memory fragments: +6. Illusion enemies (MS 75+): non-removable +5 per encounter. The Underground Laboratory contains no Mind State reduction options. Stock Blue Gems before entry.</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="segments-section">
          <div class="segments-label">Map Segments</div>
          <div class="segments-grid">
            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Old-Laboratory-scaled.png" 
			alt="Laboratory" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Old Laboratory (Decayed)</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Old Laboratory (Decayed)</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">The original experimentation chambers, now abandoned. Document cache at the Reception Ruins. The Surgical Theater triggers Memory Fragments. Hidden Records Room contains critical experiment documentation.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">Ghost Memory NPCs appear in the old section as psychic echoes of past victims, children at the ages they were when the experiments were conducted. They do not respond to interaction. They simply repeat the last thing they were doing when the experiments ended. Some of them are still waiting to be told they can go home.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Book of Pestilence (series)</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Key to Restricted Archive</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Subject Notes</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Active-Laboratory-Under-Church-Administrative-WIng-scaled.png" 
			alt="Laboratory" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Active Laboratory (New Section)</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Active Laboratory (New Section)</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">Currently operating under Priest Father Calum's leadership. Subject Holding Cells with NPCs. Active Surgical Theater as encounter point. Priest's Office holds the most important documents in the game.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">Father Calum moves through the Active Laboratory with the proprietary comfort of a man overseeing his life's work. He does not see the subjects as people. He sees them as processes, incomplete transformations in progress. This is not madness. It is the end state of a philosophy that began with a reasonable-sounding premise and was never interrupted.</div>
                <div class="segment-items">
                  <div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div>
                  <div class="segment-items-list">
                    <span class="item-tag">Critical Experiment Documents</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Antibiotics / Betadine</span>
                    <span class="item-tag">Red Vials</span>
                  </div>
                </div>
              </div>
            </div>

            <div class="segment-card reveal">
              <div class="segment-img-wrap">
			<img 
			src="http://elucidate.unaux.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/Subterranean-Labyrithn-Slowed-Movements-Connected-to-New-Laboratory-Theocratic-Capital-scaled.png" 
			alt="Laboratory" 
			class="region-img">
                <div class="segment-img-overlay"></div>
                <div class="segment-img-label">Connecting Passage</div>
              </div>
              <div class="segment-body">
                <div class="segment-name">Connecting Passage</div>
                <div class="segment-desc">The transitional corridor between old and new sections. A flooding corridor serves as environmental hazard. Guard checkpoints staffed by Priest's soldiers. One-way emergency exit for certain paths.</div>
                <div class="segment-lore">The flooding in the corridor occurs at regular intervals, a drainage failure that has been documented and unfixed for six years. The maintenance report sits in the Priest's Office, filed under a category that means it will not be reviewed until the quarterly inspection, which is nine months away, which suggests the Priest has decided that flooding is an acceptable condition for a corridor used only by people who are already imprisoned.</div>
                <div class="segment-items"><div class="segment-items-label">Items Found</div><div class="segment-items-list"><span class="item-tag">Red Vials</span></div></div>
              </div>
            </div>

        <div class="items-found reveal">
          <div class="items-found-title">All Items Found in Underground Laboratories</div>
